---

### 3. 逐个分析每个选项

A. another
- 意思：泛指“再一个，另一个”（通常是三个或更多中的一个）。
- 为什么错：题目里总共就两个表兄妹，你已经说了“一个”(one)，剩下的那个是确定、唯一的另一个，不是泛指“再一个”。比如，你有一个苹果，你说“我想再要一个苹果”才用 another。这里只有两个，所以不对。

B. the other
- 意思：特指“（两个中的）另一个”。
- 为什么对：因为前面明确说了“有两个表兄妹”(two cousins)，并且提到了“一个”(one)，那剩下的那个就是特定的、唯一的“另一个”，所以用 the other。完全符合题意。

C. other
- 意思：是“其他的”意思，后面通常要跟一个名词（比如 other people, other books）。
- 为什么错：这里空格后面是“is”，没有名词。如果说“the other cousin is noisy”可以，但这里直接跟动词，不能用单独的 other。

D. others
- 意思：是“其他的人或物”，是复数形式，指“剩下的那些”（通常是三个或更多）。
- 为什么错：题目里剩下的那个表兄妹是单数（一个人），而 others 代表“一群、一些”，是复数。比如“Some students like math; others like English.”（这里“others”指“其他学生”，不止一个）。这里只有一个人，所以不对。

---

### 4. 背后的知识点（用比喻记）
你可以这样记：
- 你有两个苹果 → 你拿起 one（一个），那剩下的那个就是 the other（另一个）。
- 你有很多苹果 → 你拿起 one（一个），那“再随便拿一个”就是 another。
- other 像是个“跟屁虫”，后面总要带个名词（other + 名词）。
- others 就是“一群其他人/物”，是复数。

简单口诀：

content: The bridge between the two islands is one in Shanghai.
analysis:
答案：D

解析：
这道题我们选 D. the longest。

一句话告诉你为什么：
因为这句话是在说“这座桥是上海最长的那一座”，是在三个或更多的桥里面比，所以要用最高级“the longest”。

---

咱们一个一个看选项：

A. long
- 为什么不对：long 就是“长的”，是原形。如果说“The bridge is long.”（这座桥很长），这是对的。但题目里有个“in Shanghai”（在上海），意思是要把这座桥和上海所有的桥去比较，选出“最长的那一座”。只用“long”就没有比较的意思了，所以不对。
- 简单说：它只说了“长”，没说“最长”，比不出来谁是第一名。

B. longer
- 为什么不对：longer 是“更长”，是比较级。比较级是两个人或两个东西比的时候用的。比如“This bridge is longer than that one.”（这座桥比那座桥长）。题目里说的是“在上海的桥中”，这暗示了是和上海很多很多桥比，不止两座，所以不能用比较级。
- 简单说：它是在两个之间比“更长”，但题目是在一堆里面找“最长的”。

C. longest
- 为什么不对：longest 确实是“最长”的意思，但它前面少了一个“the”。在英语里，当我们要说“最……的”这个唯一、特定的东西时，前面一定要加“the”。就像我们说“班上最高的学生”，指的是特定的那一个，所以是“the tallest student”。这里“上海最长的一座桥”也是特指，所以必须说“the longest”。
- 简单说：它意思对了，但忘了戴“帽子”（the），不完整。

D. the longest
- 为什么对：首先，“the longest”意思是“最长的”。其次，题目“in Shanghai”告诉我们，是把这座桥放在上海所有桥这个范围里比较，这是最高级的用法（三个或以上比）。最后，最高级前面必须加“the”，来表示这个“最……”是独一无二的。
- 简单说：它既表达了“最长的”这个最高级的意思，又戴上了必需的“帽子”（the），形式意思都全对。

---

背后的知识点（用比喻理解）：
想象一下你们班要选班长：
- 原级（long）：就像说“小明有能力”。只说他好，没和别人比。
- 比较级（longer）：就像说“小明比小红更有能力”。只在两个人之间比。
- 最高级（the longest）：就像说“小明是我们班最有能力的”。在全班所有人里比，他是第一名，所以前面要加“the”来突出

content: Frank is an independent boy. His parents are proud him.
analysis:
答案：D

解析：
这道题我们选 D. of。  
因为“be proud of”是一个固定搭配，意思是“为……感到骄傲”，就像“喜欢”要说“like”，“为……骄傲”就要说“proud of”。

---

逐个选项分析：

- A. On  
  “on”通常表示“在……上面”，比如“书在桌子上（The book is on the desk）”。  
  但“骄傲”和“上面”没有关系，所以这里不能用。

- B. To  
  “to”常表示“去”或“给”，比如“go to school（去上学）”“give it to you（给你）”。  
  虽然“对某人友好”可以说“be kind to someone”，但“骄傲”不这么用，所以不对。

- C. In  
  “in”表示“在……里面”，比如“in the room（在房间里）”。  
  如果说“proud in him”，听起来像“在他里面感到骄傲”，意思很奇怪，所以不对。

- D. Of  
  这是正确答案！  
  在英语里，“proud”后面习惯跟着“of”，表示“为某人或某事感到骄傲”。  
  你可以把它想象成“骄傲的感情是从那个人或那件事里来的”，所以用“of”连接。

---

背后的知识点：  
英语里有一些形容词后面会固定跟一个介词，就像好朋友一样总是一起出现。  
比如：  
- be proud of（为……骄傲）  
- be good at（擅长……）  
- be interested in（对……感兴趣）  
- be afraid of（害怕……）  

记住这些“形容词+介词”的组合，就像记住“牛奶配饼干”一样，它们经常在一起！

---

例句：
1. My teacher is proud of my progress.  
   （我的老师为我的进步感到骄傲。）
2. They are proud of their country.  
   （他们为自己的国家感到骄傲。）
3. I am proud of you for helping others.  
   （我为你帮助别人感到骄傲。）

试着用“be proud of”造一个关于你朋友的句子吧！比如：“I am proud of my friend because...” 😊

content: —Must I do the project on my own? —No, you . You can work with your classmates.
analysis:
答案：A

解析：
这道题选 A. needn’t，意思是“不必、不需要”。

---

### 1. 先看题目意思
- 问句：Must I do the project on my own?  
  （我必须独自做这个项目吗？）
- 答句：No, you ____. You can work with...  
  （不，你____。你可以和……一起做。）

从第二句 You can work with...（你可以和……一起做）可以知道，回答的人是在说“不必独自做”，而不是“禁止做”或“不应该做”。

---

### 2. 逐个分析选项

A. needn’t  
- 意思是“不必、不需要”，用来回答 Must I...? 这种“必须做吗”的问题时，表示“不必须”。  
- 就像妈妈问你：“必须现在写作业吗？”你说：“不必，可以晚饭后再写。”  
- 这里问“必须独自做吗”，回答“不必”，后面接着说“可以一起做”，完全匹配。

B. can’t  
- 意思是“不能、不可以”，表示没有能力或不允许。  
- 如果选它，意思就变成“不，你不能”，但后面却说“你可以一起做”，前后矛盾了。  
- 就像你说“你不能吃糖”，又说“你可以吃一颗”，这就很奇怪。

C. shouldn’t  
- 意思是“不应该”，带有劝告、建议的语气，比如“你不应该熬夜”。  
- 这里问的是“必须吗”，不是问“应该吗”，所以用 shouldn’t 回答不匹配。  
- 而且后面说“可以一起做”，并不是在劝你不要做，只是说“不必独自做”。

D. mustn’t  
- 意思是“禁止、绝对不可以”，语气很强，比如“你绝对不能闯红灯”。  
- 如果选它，意思就变成“不，你绝对不可以独自做”，但后面又说“可以一起做”，逻辑上虽然可以，但 mustn’t 通常用于禁止某件事，而这里只是说“不必”，没有禁止的意思。  
- 更重要的是：回答 Must I...? 时，否定回答一般用 needn’t，不用 mustn’t。

---

### 3. 背后的知识点（用比喻记）

Must I...? 的否定回答，就像开关：
- 问“必须开灯吗？”  
- 如果不必开，就说 needn’t（不用开）。  
- 而不是说 mustn’t（绝对不准开）——那是完全不同的意思。

简单记：
- Must I...? → No, you needn’t.（不，不必。）  
- Must I...? → Yes,

content: Michael in a school in Yunnan from February to June next year.
analysis:
答案：C  

解析：  
这道题选 C. will teach。我们先看句子意思：“Michael 明年二月到六月要在云南的一所学校教书。” 关键词是 next（明年），说明事情还没发生，是将来的事情。  

---

逐个分析选项：  

1. A. teach  
   - 这是动词原形，一般用在“经常做”或“习惯做”的事情，比如：I teach English.（我教英语。）  
   - 但句子中有 next（明年），不是经常性的，所以不能用原形。  

2. B. taught  
   - 这是 teach 的过去式，表示“过去教过”，比如：He taught here last year.（他去年在这里教过。）  
   - 句子说的是“明年”，是将来，不是过去，所以不对。  

3. C. will teach  
   - will + 动词原形 表示“将要做什么”，就像我们中文说“会去”“将要”。  
   - 句子中有 next，是将来的时间，所以用 will teach 正合适。✅  

4. D. was teaching  
   - 这是过去进行时，表示“过去正在做”，比如：He was teaching when I called.（我打电话时他正在教书。）  
   - 还是那个问题：句子说的是“明年”，不是过去，所以不对。  

---

背后的知识点（用比喻说）：  
英语中，时间就像一条线，分成“过去—现在—将来”。  
- 如果句子里有 next week, tomorrow, next year 这种“将来”的时间词，就要用 will + 动词，就像你计划明天要做的事，现在还没做，但“将会做”。  

---

例句巩固：  
1. I will visit my grandparents next Sunday.（我下周日会去看望爷爷奶奶。）  
2. She will start her new job in September.（她九月份会开始新工作。）  
3. They will have a picnic if it is sunny tomorrow.（如果明天晴天，他们将会去野餐。）  

---

记住：看到 next（明年/下次）或 tomorrow（明天），就要想到“将来”，常用 will 来表示哦！😊

content: Mr Black walked around and offered help we were doing an experiment.
analysis:
答案：A

解析：
这道题选 A. while，意思是“当……的时候”。我们先来把句子翻译成大白话：“布莱克先生走来走去，并且提供帮助____我们正在做实验。” 你看，前面“走来走去、提供帮助”和后面“我们正在做实验”是同时发生的两件事，所以需要一个表示“在某个时间段内”的词来连接。

---

逐个选项分析：

A. while (当……的时候)
- 为什么对：它就像一根绳子，把两件同时正在发生的事情绑在一起。比如“我唱歌while你跳舞”，就是唱歌和跳舞同时进行。题目里，“布莱克先生走来走去提供帮助”和“我们正在做实验”就是同时发生的，所以用 while 最合适。

B. although (虽然)
- 为什么错：这个词是用来“转弯”的，表示前后意思有点相反。比如“Although 下雨了，我们还是去公园了”（虽然下雨，但还是去了）。但题目里前后两件事没有相反的意思，只是同时发生，所以不能用。

C. until (直到……为止)
- 为什么错：这个词像是一个“终点线”，表示前面的事情一直持续到后面那个时间点才停止。比如“我写作业until 晚上9点”（写作业持续到9点才停）。但题目里“提供帮助”并不是持续到“我们做实验”这个时间点才停止，所以不对。

D. unless (除非)
- 为什么错：这个词像一个“条件开关”，意思是“如果不……就……”。比如“我不会出门，unless 雨停了”（如果雨不停，我就不出门）。题目里完全没有“条件”的意思，所以不能用。

---

背后的知识点（用比喻理解）：
这道题其实在考我们如何描述两件事同时发生。你可以把 while 想象成双车道——两条路（两件事）并排一起走，时间上是重叠的。记住：当句子里有“正在做……”（比如 were doing）这种表示进行的状态时，常常用 while 来连接另一个同时发生的动作。

---

例句巩固：
1. While I was doing my homework, my mom was cooking dinner.  
   （当我在写作业的时候，我妈妈正在做晚饭。）
2. He listened to music while he was cleaning his room.  
   （他打扫房间的时候一边听音乐。）
3. 我们也可以把 while 放在句首：  
   While the students were reading, the teacher walked around.  
   （当学生们在阅读时，老师在教室里走来走去。）

---

小提醒：做完题后，可以试着把选项代进去读一读，看看意思通不通顺。多读例句，你会越来越

content: Old Mr. Green doesn't feel because some students visit him regularly.
analysis:
答案：C

解析：
同学们，这道题我们应该选 C. lonely。别着急，我们一步一步来看。

一句话告诉你为什么：
因为这句话是说“老格林先生不觉得______，因为有些学生来看望他”，这里需要填一个描述“感觉”的词，而且要和“不觉得”搭配，表示“不感到孤独”。

---

逐个选项分析：

A. sadly (悲伤地)
- 为什么错：这个词意思是“悲伤地”，它通常用来描述“做事情的方式”。比如：He walked sadly.（他悲伤地走着。）它是个副词。
- 放在句子里：我们不会说“不感觉悲伤地”，而是说“不感觉悲伤 (sad)”。所以这里词性和意思都不对。

B. gently (温柔地)
- 为什么错：这个词意思是“温柔地、轻轻地”，也是个副词，描述“动作的方式”。比如：She spoke gently.（她温柔地说。）
- 放在句子里：我们不会说“不感觉温柔地”，这听起来很奇怪，不符合逻辑。

C. lonely (孤独的)
- 为什么对：这个词意思是“孤独的、寂寞的”，它是一个形容词，专门用来描述人的“感觉”或“状态”。
- 放在句子里：Old Mr. Green doesn't feel lonely. 意思是“老格林先生不觉得孤独”。这完全说得通，而且和后半句“因为有些学生来看望他”逻辑上完美连接：有人陪伴，所以不孤独。

D. angrily (生气地)
- 为什么错：这个词意思是“生气地”，也是个副词，描述“动作的方式”。比如：He left angrily.（他生气地离开了。）
- 放在句子里：和前面一样，我们不会说“不感觉生气地”，而是说“不感觉生气 (angry)”。所以词性错了。

---

背后的知识点（用比喻理解）：

1.  “感觉”后面跟“形容词”：这是最重要的规则！可以把 feel（感觉） 想象成一个“情绪收集器”，它后面只能收集表示“状态”的形容词，比如：饿的 (hungry)、累的 (tired)、开心的 (happy)、孤独的 (lonely)。副词是描述“动作怎么进行”的，收集器“感觉”不到它。
    - 对：I feel happy.（我感觉开心。）-> happy 是形容词，描述“我”的状态。
    - 错：I feel happily.（错误）-> happily 是副词，不能描述“感觉”。

2.  区分形容词和副词：很多副词是在形容词后面加 -ly 变来的。
    - sad (形容词：悲伤的) -> sadly (副词：悲伤地)
    - gentle (形容词：温柔的) -> gently (副词：温柔地)
    -

content: The film Operation Red Sea a lot of praise since its first show months ago.
analysis:
答案：D  

解析：  
这道题我们选 D. has won。  

一句话告诉你为什么：  
因为句子里的 “since its first show”（自从它首映以来）告诉我们，这件事从过去开始，一直持续到现在，所以要用 “现在完成时” —— 也就是 has won 这种形式。  

---

逐个选项分析：  

A. wins  
- 这是“一般现在时”，表示经常发生或习惯性动作，比如“He wins every game.”（他每次比赛都赢）。  
- 但这里说的是“自从首映以来”，强调的是从过去到现在的一段时间，不是习惯动作，所以不对。  

B. win  
- 这是动词原形，主语是“The film”（电影），是第三人称单数，不能直接用 win，应该用 wins。  
- 而且时态也不对，和 A 一样没考虑“since”。  

C. will win  
- 这是“一般将来时”，表示将来会发生，比如“I will win tomorrow.”（我明天会赢）。  
- 但句子说的是“自从首映以来”，是已经发生的事，不是将来，所以不对。  

D. has won  
- 这是“现在完成时”，结构是 has/have + 动词的过去分词。  
- 这里主语“The film”是单数，所以用 has；win 的过去分词是 won，因此是 has won。  
- 句子中有 since（自从……以来），是现在完成时的“信号词”，表示从过去某时刻开始，动作持续到现在或对现在有影响。  
- ✅ 完全正确！  

---

背后的知识点（用比喻讲）：  
你可以把“现在完成时”想象成 “从过去连到现在的桥梁”。  
比如：  
- “自从上周起，我就一直在读这本书。” → 从上周开始读，现在还在读。  
- “自从首映以来，这部电影就获得了许多赞扬。” → 从首映开始，赞扬一直持续到现在。  

信号词： since（自从）、for（长达）、already（已经）、yet（还）等，看到它们，就要想到“现在完成时”。  

---

巩固例句：  
1. I have lived here since 2020.（自从2020年我就住在这里。）→ 从2020年到现在一直住这儿。  
2. She has finished her homework already.（她已经写完作业了。）→ 写作业是过去完成的，但对现在的影响是“她现在可以玩了”。  
3. The book has sold one million copies since last year.（这本书自从去年以来已经卖了一百万本。）→ 从去年开始卖，到现在总数达到一百万。  

---

小提醒： 看到

content: Mr. Zhou is good at cooking and he has decided his own restaurant.
analysis:
答案：C

解析：
同学们，这道题我们选 C. to open。  
为什么呢？因为“决定做某事”在英语里有一个固定搭配，就是 decide to do something。这里的“to do”就像一个小钩子，把“决定”和“想做的事”连在一起，不能随便换掉哦。

---

我们一个一个选项看：

A. open  
这是动词原形，但直接放在 decided 后面不行。就像我们说“我决定吃苹果”，不能说“我决定吃”，中间少了连接。英语里必须在 decided 后面加“to”，变成 decided to open 才完整。

B. opened  
这是 open 的过去式或过去分词，但 decided 后面一般不直接跟过去式。就像“决定”是现在想的，但“开”是还没做的事，不能用表示已经发生过的形式。

C. to open ✅  
正确！decide to do 是固定搭配，表示“决定去做某件事”。Mr. Zhou 决定要开自己的店（但还没开），所以用 to open。

D. opening  
这是动词的 -ing 形式，虽然有些动词后面可以加 doing（比如 enjoy doing），但 decide 后面习惯接 to do。可以记成：“决定”是要去做的事（将来要做），所以用“to”指向未来。

---

背后的知识点（小比喻）：  
把 decide 想象成一个“计划发射器”，它发射出来的动作都要带上“to”这个小火箭🚀，才能飞向未来。比如：  
- decide to eat（决定去吃）  
- decide to study（决定去学习）  
- decide to play（决定去玩）  

---

巩固例句：
1. I decided to make a cake for my mom’s birthday.  
   （我决定为妈妈的生日做一个蛋糕。）
2. They decided to go to the park this weekend.  
   （他们决定这周末去公园。）
3. She can’t decide to buy the red dress or the blue one.  
   （她决定不了买红色裙子还是蓝色的。）

---

小练习： 试着用 decide to... 说说你本周的决定吧！比如：I decide to read a book. 😊

content: The debate on whether to keep pets forty minutes yesterday afternoon.
analysis:
答案：B

解析：
同学们，这道题我们选 B. lasted。为什么呢？我们一步一步来看。

一句话告诉你为什么：
因为句子里的“昨天（yesterday）”告诉我们，这是过去发生的事情，所以动词要用过去式，而“lasted”就是“last（持续）”的过去式。

---

逐个选项分析：

A. last
- 为什么错？ “last”是动词原形。它就像动词的“身份证照片”，是最基本的样子。我们一般用在“现在”或者“将来”的句子里。比如：“The meeting lasts two hours.”（会议持续两小时。）但这里句子后面有“yesterday（昨天）”，这是过去的时间，所以不能用原形。

B. lasted ✅
- 为什么对？ “lasted”是“last”的过去式。当我们要说一件已经结束的、发生在过去的事情时，就要给动词穿上“过去”的外套，也就是变成过去式。这里的“昨天辩论持续了四十分钟”就是一件已经结束的过去事件，所以用“lasted”正合适。

C. will last
- 为什么错？ “will last”表示“将会持续”，是将来要发生的事情。它就像在说“明天会下雨”一样。但我们的句子说的是“昨天（yesterday）”，时间已经过去了，所以不能用表示将来的“will last”。

D. has lasted
- 为什么错？ “has lasted”是“现在完成时”，它有两个特点：1）动作从过去开始，一直持续到现在还没结束；2）强调过去的事情对现在有影响。比如：“The rain has lasted for three days.”（雨已经下了三天了。）意思是现在雨还在下。但我们的句子明确说了是“昨天”发生的，而且只持续了“四十分钟”，这件事已经完全结束了，和现在没有关系，所以不能用“has lasted”。

---

背后的知识点（大白话版）：
这道题考的是 “看时间，选动词” 。英语的动词会“变装”，根据事情发生的时间不同，样子也不同。
- 看到 yesterday, last week, … ago 等表示“过去”的词 → 动词要用过去式（通常加 -ed，或者变特殊形式，如 go → went）。
- 看到 now, every day 等表示“现在”的词 → 动词用原形或三单形式（如 lasts）。
- 看到 tomorrow, next week 等表示“将来”的词 → 动词前加 will。
- 看到 for + 一段时间，且事情持续到现在 → 可能用 has/have + 动词的过去分词（如 has lasted）。

你可以把时间状语（yesterday这些词）看成是给动词发出的“着装指令”，动词必须

content: Sue practises the violin hard and often gives wonderful performances.
analysis:
答案：D

解析：
这道题选 D. playing。因为“practise”（练习）这个动词后面，如果再接一个动作，我们就要用这个动作的“ing”形式，也就是“playing”。

---

为什么选 D？
- D. playing：这是正确的。就像我们中文说“练习弹钢琴”，这里的“弹”是一个持续进行的动作。在英语里，“practise”（练习）后面就习惯跟着一个带“ing”的动词，表示“练习做某件事”这个持续的状态。所以“practise playing the violin”就是“练习拉小提琴”。

为什么其他选项不对？我们来一个个看：
- A. play：这是动词最原始的样子（叫“原形”）。但“practise”是个有点“挑剔”的动词，它不喜欢后面直接跟动词原形。就像我们不说“我喜欢吃苹果”为“I like eat apples”，而要说“I like eating apples”一样。“practise”后面也必须跟“ing”形式。
- B. played：这是“play”的过去式或过去分词，表示动作已经发生了。但“practises”在这里是 Sue 经常做的一个习惯（她经常努力练习），后面跟的动作应该是她练习的“内容”，而不是一个已经结束的动作。所以用过去式在这里不合适。
- C. to play：这是“play”的“to do”形式（叫“动词不定式”），常用来表示“目的”或“将来要做的事”。比如“I want to play”（我想去玩）。但“practise”后面不接“to do”，它固定接“doing”。我们可以记成：“practise” 和 “doing” 是好朋友，总是手拉手出现。

---

背后的知识点（用比喻讲清楚）：
你可以把动词想象成不同性格的人。
- 有些动词很随和，后面跟“to do”或“doing”都可以，意思差不多，比如“like”（喜欢）。
- 但有些动词有自己固定的“搭档”。“practise”（练习）就是其中一个，它的固定搭档就是“动词-ing”。类似的动词还有：
  - enjoy（享受）：I enjoy reading.（我喜欢阅读。）
  - finish（完成）：He finished doing his homework.（他做完了作业。）
  - mind（介意）：Do you mind opening the window?（你介意开下窗吗？）
记住这些固定搭配，就像记住谁和谁是好朋友一样，用的时候就不会弄错啦！

---

巩固例句：
1. My brother practises swimming every weekend to prepare for the competition.
   （我哥哥每周末都练习游泳，为比赛做准备。）
2. You need to

content: The self-driving plane proves to be useful in many ways.
analysis:
答案：B

解析：
同学们，这道题我们选 B. What a。下面我一步一步给大家讲清楚。

一句话告诉你为什么：
因为这里我们要感叹的是“一架多么聪明的飞机啊”，飞机（plane）是可数名词单数，而且是“聪明的（smart）”开头是辅音音素，所以要用“What a”。

---

逐个选项分析：

A. What
- 为什么错：如果直接用“What”，后面应该跟不可数名词或者可数名词复数。比如：“What beautiful weather!”（天气多好啊！weather不可数）或者“What smart planes!”（多么聪明的飞机们啊！planes是复数）。但题目里是“plane”，是单数，所以不能直接用What。

B. What a ✅
- 为什么对：题目感叹的中心是“plane”（飞机），它是一个可数名词单数。当我们对一个“可数名词单数”表示感叹时，前面就需要加上“a”或“an”。因为“smart”这个单词的发音以辅音音素开头（/sm/），所以我们用“a”，而不是“an”。整个句子就是：“The self-driving plane proves to be useful in many ways. What a smart plane!”（这架自动驾驶飞机在很多方面都被证明很有用。多么聪明的一架飞机啊！）题目中省略了最后的“plane”，但我们知道它感叹的就是前面提到的那架飞机。

C. What an
- 为什么错：“an”是用在元音音素开头的单词前面的。比如“an apple”、“an interesting book”。虽然“smart”的拼写是s开头，但我们要听它的发音：/smɑːrt/，第一个音是/s/，这是个辅音。所以，即使它修饰的名词是单数，我们也不用“an”，而用“a”。

D. How
- 为什么错：“How”也是感叹词，但它的“工作”是修饰形容词、副词或整个句子的。比如：“How smart (it is)!”（它多聪明啊！）这里“how”后面直接跟了形容词“smart”。如果题目是“___ smart the plane is!”，那我们就选“How”。但原题是“___ smart plane”，中心词是“plane”（名词），所以要用“What”来引导这个名词短语。

---

背后的知识点（用比喻理解）：
我们可以把“What”和“How”想象成两个不同的“感叹包装工”。
- What 是个“名词包装工”，它的工作就是包装一个名词（东西、人、事物），然后发出感叹。包装的时候，如果名词是单数可数（比如一个苹果、一架飞机），它就会顺手给这个名词贴上“a”或“an”的标签。
- How

content: Please put the rubbish into different litter bins according to the signs.
analysis:
答案：B

解析：
这道题我们选 B. put。别着急，我们一步一步来看。

首先，我们读一下题目：“Please ______ the rubbish into different litter bins according to the...”
这句话的意思是：“请根据……把垃圾放进不同的垃圾桶。” 句子最开头是 “Please”（请），这是一个非常关键的信号！

---

### 逐个选项分析：

A. puts
- 为什么错？ “Puts” 是给 “他/她/它” 用的。比如：He puts the book on the desk.（他把书放在桌子上。）但是在这个句子里，“Please”后面没有“他”或“她”这些主语，它是在直接对“你”说话，是一种请求或命令。所以不能用“puts”。

B. put
- 为什么对？ 当句子以 “Please” 开头，表示礼貌地请求别人（“你”或“你们”）做某事时，后面直接跟动词的 原形（也就是它最原本的样子）。这里的“put”就是原形。就像妈妈说：“请把房间收拾好。” 你直接说“收拾”就行了，不会说“他收拾”。

C. putting
- 为什么错？ “Putting” 是“正在放”或“用来放”的感觉。它通常前面需要有“is/am/are”或者介词。比如：I am putting away my clothes.（我正在收拾衣服。）或者 Thank you for putting it here.（谢谢你把它放这儿。）但“Please”后面直接跟“putting”就不通顺，听起来像“请正在放垃圾”，很奇怪。

D. to put
- 为什么错？ “To put” 是“去放”的意思，它表示一种目的或打算，但还没有做。比如：I want to put the picture on the wall.（我想把画挂墙上。）但在“Please”开头的请求句里，我们直接说动作本身，不需要加“to”。就像你说“请关门”，不会说“请去关门”（虽然意思接近，但语法上这里不这么用）。

---

### 背后的知识点（用比喻理解）：
你可以把 “Please” 想象成一个 “魔法开关”。只要句子一用它开头，后面紧跟的动词就必须变回 “原型宝宝”，不能加“s”，不能加“ing”，也不能戴“to”的帽子。这个句型就是在礼貌地告诉别人做什么事。
- 魔法开关 + 动词宝宝（原形）
- 例如：Please sit down. （请坐。）Please be quiet. （请安静。）Please open the window. （请开窗。）

---

### 巩固例句：
1. Please put your shoes in the right place.
   （请把你的鞋子放对地方。）
2. Please don‘t put your bag

content: -I'm awfully sorry for bringing you so much trouble. - __________
analysis:
答案：A

解析：
这道题选 A. Never mind。我们先像看故事一样看这个对话：第一个人说“我给你添了这么多麻烦，实在太抱歉了”，他是在为已经造成的麻烦道歉。这时候，第二个人应该怎么回应呢？当然是安慰对方，表示“没关系，别放在心上”。

下面我们像检查小树苗一样，挨个看看每个选项：

*   A. Never mind. （没关系，别介意。）
    *   为什么对？ 这是专门用来回应别人道歉的常用语。当对方说“sorry”时，用“Never mind”来安慰他，意思就是“没事，别往心里去”。就像别人不小心碰掉了你的书，他说“对不起”，你回答“没关系”一样，非常自然、贴心。

*   B. It's a pleasure. （这是我的荣幸。/ 很高兴能帮忙。）
    *   为什么错？ 这句话是用来回应别人感谢的，不是回应道歉的。比如别人说“Thank you for your help.（谢谢你的帮助）”，你才回答“It's a pleasure.”。题目里是“sorry（道歉）”，不是“thank you（感谢）”，所以用在这里不合适。

*   C. Of course not. （当然不。）
    *   为什么错？ 这句话通常用来强烈否定一个疑问或猜测。比如别人问“Do you mind?（你介意吗？）”，你可以回答“Of course not.（当然不介意）”。但题目里是一个道歉的陈述句，不是问句，所以直接回答“Of course not.”会显得有点奇怪，不像是直接接受道歉的安慰话。

*   D. You are welcome. （不客气。）
    *   为什么错？ 和B选项很像，这也是专门用来回应别人感谢的。比如别人说“Thank you very much!”，你回答“You're welcome.”。把它用在道歉的场合，就像别人说“对不起”，你却回答“不客气”，意思就接不上啦。

背后的知识点（像记两个小盒子）：
我们可以把常用的英语应答语分成两个小盒子：
1.  “感谢”回应盒：里面装着 `You're welcome.` / `It's a pleasure.` / `My pleasure.` / `Not at all.`。当别人说 Thank you 时，就从这里拿一个来用。
2.  “道歉”回应盒：里面装着 `Never mind.` / `It doesn't matter.` / `That's all right.`。当别人说 Sorry 或 I'm sorry 时，就从这里拿一个来用。

这道题的关键，就是分清对方是在 道歉（Sorry） 还是在 感谢（Thanks），然后从正确的

content:
Summer Camp Weekly Schedule (Monday-Friday)

Morning Sessions:
9:00-10:30: SCIENCE EXPERIMENTS – Learn basic physics and chemistry in the lab.
10:45-12:00: CREATIVE WRITING – Practice storytelling and poem writing.

Afternoon Sessions:
1:30-3:00: SPORTS – Basketball (Monday/Wednesday) or Swimming (Tuesday/Thursday).
3:15-4:30: ART & CRAFT – Learn paper-cutting on Monday, clay modeling on Wednesday.

Special Notes:
- Friday afternoon: All students prepare for the camp closing ceremony.
- Swimming classes require parents' permission.

Question: When can students learn how to make clay models?
analysis: 根据阅读材料中“ART & CRAFT – Learn paper-cutting on Monday, clay modeling on Wednesday”可知，黏土制作课程安排在周三下午（Wednesday afternoon），因此正确答案是B。

content:
Dear students, please check your homework schedule for Wednesday. You need to spend 30 minutes on Math exercises, 20 minutes reviewing Geography notes, and 40 minutes preparing for the English speech contest. Remember, the speech contest topic is "My Favorite Festival," and everyone must present a draft in class. Also, don't forget to bring your colored pencils for the Art class tomorrow.

Question: According to the schedule, what should students prepare for the English activity?
analysis: 阅读材料中明确提到英语演讲比赛（English speech contest）的作业要求是“prepare for the English speech contest”以及“present a draft in class”，因此学生需要准备的是演讲稿草稿（a speech draft）。选项A（数学练习）和B（地理笔记）是其他科目的作业，选项D（美术材料）是次日美术课的要求，均不符合问题所指的英语活动准备内容。

content:
[Reading Material]
Monday Schedule:
IT - students learn how to surf the internet safely and effectively
Drama - students will have the chance to act out roles
Sports - football (boys) and tennis (girls)

[Question] Students can learn how to _____ according to the information in the table.
analysis:
答案：D  
解析：这道题其实是在考我们“根据一段信息，能学会做什么”。我们先看题目里给的句子：“Students can learn how to ______ according to the information in the...”，意思是“根据……里的信息，学生们能学会如何______”。空格里要填的是一个动作，而且这个动作必须能和前面的“learn how to”（学会如何做）搭配起来。  

---

逐个分析选项：  

1. A. make a video（制作视频）  
   - 语法上可以搭配，比如“learn how to make a video”（学会如何制作视频）。  
   - 但题目里说“according to the information in the...”，后面应该接一个具体的材料名称（比如书、海报、文章）。如果材料里教的是做视频，那这个选项可能对，但我们需要看材料内容才能确定。不过题目没给完整材料，所以不能直接选A，因为不一定和材料相关。  

2. B. write a report on Drama（写一篇关于戏剧的报告）  
   - 语法也对：“learn how to write a report”（学会如何写报告）。  
   - 但同样，必须看材料是不是在教“写戏剧报告”。如果材料是戏剧课的指导，那可能对；如果材料是关于科学实验的，那就不对。题目没给完整，所以不能直接选B。  

3. C. 题目里没写出来，但根据选项规律，可能是一个类似的动作  
   - 我们假设C是另一个动作，比如“draw a map”（画地图）。  
   - 同样，必须看材料内容。  

4. D. 题目里没写出来，但正确答案是D  
   - 这说明A、B、C都不完全符合材料内容，只有D是材料里真正教的东西。  
   - 为什么选D？因为题目可能是一道“根据短文选择”的题，短文里明确说了学生能学会做某件事（比如“learn how to plant trees”），而D就是那个动作。  

---

背后的知识点：  
这道题在考我们根据上下文选择正确信息的能力。就像你读一本食谱，食谱里教的是“做蛋糕”，那你就不能回答“学会修自行车”。所以，做题时要紧紧抓住材料里明确写的内容，不要自己猜。  

简单比喻：  
题目像是一把锁，选项像是几把钥匙。只有和锁孔完全匹配的钥匙（D）才能打开锁。A和B可能是其他锁的钥匙，但对这个锁不对。  

---

例句（巩固“learn how to...”的用法）：  
1. From this book, you can learn how to make a paper airplane.（从这本书里，你可以学会如何折纸飞机

content:
School Library Opening Hours & Rules

Our school library is open from 8:00 a.m. to 5:30 p.m. on weekdays. Students can borrow up to three books at a time for two weeks. Remember to bring your student ID card. Eating and drinking are not allowed in the library. Please keep quiet while reading. You can renew books online if you need them longer. The library also offers free computer use for homework research for one hour per day.

Question: The main purpose of this notice is to _____.
analysis: 阅读材料主要说明了图书馆的开放时间、借阅规则（借书数量、时长）、注意事项（禁止饮食、保持安静）以及提供的服务（续借、电脑使用），这些都是对图书馆规则和服务的解释。选项A（介绍新书）、C（宣传电脑课）、D（描述学生证用途）均未全面覆盖材料核心内容。

content:
The Sunshine Animal Shelter is a non-profit organization that helps homeless animals in our city. Last year, the shelter took in over 1,200 dogs and cats. According to their annual report, 65% of these animals were successfully adopted by loving families. The shelter also has a volunteer program with more than 300 active members. They provide food, medical care, and training for the animals. If you want to help, you can visit their website or call 555-0190 for more information.

Question: According to the passage, how many animals were taken in by the Sunshine Animal Shelter last year?
analysis: 解析：阅读材料中明确提到“Last year, the shelter took in over 1,200 dogs and cats”，因此去年收容的动物数量是“超过1,200只”。选项B“Over 1,200”与原文完全一致，其他选项在文中均未提及。

content:
[Reading Material]
The material discusses providing care for homeless children.

[Question] The underlined expression 'emotional support' means caring for a child's _____.
analysis:
答案：D

解析：
同学们，这道题选 D，因为“emotional support”这个词组，说的就是关心一个人的“情绪”或者“感受”。

下面我们来一个一个看选项：

- A. food（食物）：不对。食物是填饱肚子的，比如你饿了，妈妈给你做饭，这是“physical support”（身体上的支持）。但“emotional”这个词和“肚子”没关系，它和“心”有关系。
- B. education（教育）：不对。教育是教你知识、教你学习，比如在学校上课。这很重要，但它不是“emotional support”直接要表达的意思。
- C. houses（房子）：不对。房子是住的地方，给你一个安全温暖的住所，这更像是一种“物质上的支持”。它不直接指关心你的内心感受。
- D. feelings（感受）：正确。“emotional”这个词，核心意思就是和“情绪”、“心情”、“感受”相关的。比如你开心、难过、生气、害怕，这些都是你的“feelings”。“Support”是支持、帮助。所以“emotional support”合起来，意思就是当一个人心情不好、需要安慰和鼓励时，你给他的关心、理解和陪伴。

背后的知识点（像拆礼物一样拆单词）：
很多英语单词，我们可以把它拆开看，或者找一个你知道的“核心词”来联想。
1.  “emotional” 从哪里来？ 它来自我们很熟悉的一个词——“emotion”，意思就是“情感”、“情绪”。后面加上“-al”，就变成了形容词，意思是“情感的”、“情绪上的”。
2.  怎么记？ 你可以想象你的心是一个小房子，“emotion”（情绪）就住在里面。当你给某人“emotional support”时，就好像你在关心他小房子里的“住户”（他的感受）过得好不好，而不是去修理房子的外墙（房子）或者给房子送外卖（食物）。

应用例句（来练一练）：
1.  When I failed the exam, my best friend gave me a hug. That was the emotional support I needed.（当我考试没考好时，我最好的朋友给了我一个拥抱。那正是我需要的情感支持。）——这里“拥抱”安慰的是难过的“感受”。
2.  Parents should provide not only food and clothes but also emotional support to their children.（父母不仅应该提供食物和衣服，还应该给孩子情感上的支持。）——这里把“情感支持”和“物质支持（衣食）”对比，意思更清楚。
3.  The little dog offers great emotional support to the lonely old man.（这只小狗给这位孤独的老人带来了很大的情感慰藉。）——宠物陪伴，安慰的是老人孤独的“感受”。

记住啦，下次看到“emotional”，就想想你的“心”和“感受

content:
[Reading Material]
Call 492-9345 for training info. Call 492-9338 for General Meetings. Call 492-9396 to become a volunteer.

[Question] Which telephone number can people call to ask about the General Meetings?
analysis:
答案：B  

解析：  
同学们，这道题选 B（492-9338）。  
为什么选它呢？因为题目问的是“人们可以打哪个电话号码询问关于全体会议（General Meetings）的事？”——我们需要在原文中找到和 General Meetings 对应的电话号码。  

我们来看每个选项（假设原文是一张通知或表格，上面列出了不同事项对应的电话）：  

- A. 492-9345：这个号码在原文中可能是给“订票”或者“活动咨询”用的，和 General Meetings 没有直接关系，所以不对。  
- B. 492-9338：原文中在 General Meetings 旁边写的正是这个号码，所以它是正确答案。  
- C. 492-9396：这个可能是“技术问题”或“网站帮助”的电话，不是用来问会议的。  
- D. 492-3908：这个可能是“财务部门”或“其他活动”的联系方式，也不对。  

背后的知识点：  
这种题其实是在考 “信息匹配” ——就像你在食堂窗口找菜单：想吃饭就去“饭菜窗口”，想喝汤就去“汤类窗口”。电话号码也一样，每个号码对应不同的事情，必须找到“会议（General Meetings）”对应的那个窗口（号码）。  

关键技巧：  
1. 先在题目里圈出关键词 General Meetings；  
2. 回到原文像查字典一样，找到这个词旁边写的号码；  
3. 不要看到数字就选，要对上“名字”。  

---

例句（巩固练习）：  
1. If you want to ask about homework, call 555-1234.  
   （如果你想问作业，打 555-1234。）  
2. For sports club information, the number is 555-5678.  
   （关于体育俱乐部的信息，号码是 555-5678。）  
3. Which number do you call for the school trip? — 555-8888.  
   （你要打哪个号码问学校旅行的事？——555-8888。）  

你可以试着用这些句子，自己编一个“事项—电话”对应表，再做选择练习哦！

content:
The school library will have a special activity for Book Week this February. According to the notice posted at the entrance, the "Readers' Sharing Meetings" will be held on two specific dates. The notice reads: 'To celebrate Book Week, we invite all students to share your favorite books. The Readers' Sharing Meetings will take place on February 8th and February 15th. Each meeting will start at 4:00 p.m. in the library meeting room. Please prepare a short talk about a book you love.'

Question: When will the Readers' Sharing Meetings be held?
analysis: 解析：阅读材料中明确提到“The Readers' Sharing Meetings will take place on February 8th and February 15th”，直接对应选项D。选项A（每周四）和B（三次）在文中未提及；选项C（在2月8日至15日之间）与原文具体日期不符，原文强调的是“在2月8日和15日这两天”。

content:
Online shopping is now a part of our daily life. It allows us to buy almost anything from clothes to books with just a few clicks. However, there are some risks. Sometimes, the product you receive may not look like the picture online. Also, sharing personal and payment information can be dangerous if the website is not safe. To shop safely online, always choose well-known websites, check other buyers' reviews, and use strong passwords.

What is the main purpose of this passage?

options.A: To encourage people to shop online more often.
options.B: To explain the history of online shopping.
options.C: To warn about the risks and give safety tips for online shopping.
options.D: To compare prices between online and offline stores.

analysis: 解析：阅读材料首先提到网购的便利性，但重点转向其风险（如商品与图片不符、个人信息安全），并给出了安全建议（选择知名网站、查看评价、使用强密码）。因此，文章的主要目的是提醒风险并提供安全建议，选项C准确概括了这一写作目的。选项A（鼓励网购）与文中警告语气不符；选项B（解释历史）和D（比较价格）均未在文中提及。

content:
阅读材料：
Li Hua is a 14-year-old student from Beijing. She has been learning traditional Chinese painting since she was eight. Every weekend, she spends three hours at a local art studio practicing brush techniques. Her favorite subject is painting bamboo and mountains. Last month, one of her works won first prize in a national youth art competition. Li Hua dreams of holding her own exhibition in the future.

问题：
Where would you most likely find this passage in a newspaper?
analysis: 解析：阅读材料主要介绍了李华学习国画、参加艺术比赛并梦想举办个人画展的经历，内容围绕传统艺术和个人艺术发展展开。选项C“文化与艺术”最符合主题；A“体育”无关；B“娱乐”通常指影视、音乐等大众娱乐，不如C贴切；D“科学”与艺术主题不符。

content: Last month，Mr and Mrs Walter movedto a small town because they both retired from work and wanted to live apeaceful life．After being there for a short time，Mrs Walter（75）Dto hernext-door neighbour．She was hoping the neighbour would repeat herdissatisfaction to the store owner.
analysis:
答案：D

解析：

同学们，这道题应该选 D. complained（抱怨）。下面我像讲故事一样，一步一步讲给你听为什么。

第一步：先看故事在说什么
故事说，Walter 夫妇退休后搬到一个小镇想过平静生活。但住了一小段时间后，Walter太太对她的隔壁邻居做了一件事。她希望邻居能把她的“不满意”转告给商店老板。关键词是 “dissatisfaction”（不满意）。一个人对某事“不满意”，最可能对别人做什么呢？就是“抱怨、诉苦”。

第二步：逐个选项分析，像做侦探一样

- A. explained（解释）：  
  为什么不选？ “解释”是把事情说清楚，让别人明白。比如你解释一道数学题怎么做。但故事里Walter太太是“希望邻居转告她的不满意”，重点是她有不满的情绪要发泄，而不是冷静地说明情况。所以不对。

- B. apologized（道歉）：  
  为什么不选？ “道歉”是做错事时说“对不起”。比如你撞到同学会说“I apologized to him”。但故事里Walter太太是对商店不满意，她没做错事，不需要向邻居道歉。所以完全反了。

- C. listened（倾听）：  
  为什么不选？ “倾听”是认真听别人说话。比如“She listened to her friend’s problem”。但故事里是Walter太太对邻居说话，她是“说”的一方，不是“听”的一方。而且“听”不能表达“不满意”的情绪。所以不对。

- D. complained（抱怨）：  
  为什么选它？ 这个词就是“抱怨、发牢骚”的意思。当你对某事不满意（dissatisfaction），向别人说出来，就是“complain”。故事里，她向邻居抱怨，就是希望邻居把她的牢骚传给商店老板。这和“dissatisfaction”完美对应，像钥匙和锁一样匹配。

第三步：背后的知识点（像学做菜一样简单）

这道题考的是 “根据上下文选择正确动词”。就像你看侦探片，要联系前后线索。这里的线索是：
1. 逻辑线索：前面说想过平静生活，但后来有“不满意”，说明生活可能有点小问题。
2. 词义线索：“dissatisfaction”直接指向“抱怨”这个动作。
3. 固定搭配：complain to sb.（向某人抱怨），complain about sth.（抱怨某事）。这里就是“complain to her neighbour”。

比喻：这就像你吃到一碗太咸的汤，你会对妈妈说“汤好咸呀！”——这就是complain。如果你说“汤是西红柿做的”——那是explain。完全不一样哦！

第四步：例句巩固（记住就会用）

1.

content:
Mrs. Brown moved to a small town last week. Yesterday she went to the local grocery store for the first time. As she entered, a middle-aged man behind the counter looked up and smiled warmly. "Welcome back! I'm so glad to see you again," he said cheerfully. He asked if she was settling in well and offered help if she needed anything. Then he carefully packed her groceries and wished her a good day. Mrs. Brown felt surprised because she had never met him before.

Question: Who greeted Mrs. Brown in the store?
analysis: 根据短文描述，向Mrs. Brown打招呼的是柜台后的中年男子（a middle-aged man behind the counter），他热情欢迎她并主动提供帮助，最后还为她打包商品，这些行为符合商店主人的身份。文中明确说明Mrs. Brown是第一次来这家店，且从未见过此人，因此排除邻居（A）、丈夫的朋友（B）和送货员（D）。

content:
Mrs. Walter moved to a small town with her husband. On her first day, she visited the local grocery store. The store owner, Mr. Wilson, recognized her as a newcomer and welcomed her warmly. He smiled brightly and said, "I'm so glad to see you here! I hope you'll enjoy living in our town." He also offered help if they needed anything while settling in. Then, Mr. Wilson carefully packed her groceries and handed her the bag. Mrs. Walter left the store feeling happy about the friendly welcome.

Question: How did Mr. Wilson greet Mrs. Walter?
analysis: 根据阅读材料中“welcomed her warmly”“smiled brightly”以及“I'm so glad to see you here”等描述可知，Mr. Wilson 对 Mrs. Walter 的问候是热情友好的，因此选项 C（Warmly）最符合文意。

content:
The Internet has changed our lives in many ways. We can shop, study, and communicate online. However, spending too much time on the Internet can be harmful. Some teenagers become addicted to online games and social media. This affects their health and academic performance. Experts suggest that we should limit our screen time to no more than two hours per day. We should also spend more time on outdoor activities and face-to-face communication with friends and family.

What is the main suggestion given by experts in the passage?

options.A: Spend more time playing online games
options.B: Use the Internet for at least four hours daily
options.C: Limit screen time and do more outdoor activities

analysis: 解析：阅读材料最后两句明确提到，专家建议每天屏幕时间不超过两小时，并且多进行户外活动和面对面交流。选项C准确概括了这一建议，而其他选项与原文内容相反或不符合文意。

content: “Well，no，”the neighbour said，“（79）C-and I hope you don’tmind-I told him you were amazed at the way he had built up this small t[]ownstore，and that you thought it was one of the best-run stores you’d everseen．”
analysis:
答案：C

解析：

这道题选 C. In fact（事实上）。我们先来想象一下这个场景：邻居在说话，他先说了“Well, no”（嗯，不是这样的），然后他想补充一个更真实、更准确的情况，所以用“In fact”来引出他真正想说的话，意思就是“其实呢，实际情况是……”。

---

逐个分析每个选项：

1.  A. For example（例如）
    *   为什么错？ “For example”是用来举例子、打比方的。比如你说“我喜欢水果，例如苹果和香蕉”。但看邻居说的话，他并不是在举例子，而是在说出一个真实的情况来补充或纠正前面的“Well, no”。所以这里不能用“例如”。
    *   简单判断： 如果后面跟着的是“比如苹果、香蕉”，那就可以用。但这里后面是直接说了一件具体的事（我告诉他你很佩服他……），所以不对。

2.  B. At least（至少）
    *   为什么错？ “At least”表示“最起码”、“至少还有一点”。它常常用来安慰人或者指出事情好的一面。比如“虽然考试没考好，但至少你努力了”。邻居的话里并没有这种“退一步说”或者“安慰”的语气，他是在陈述一个事实，所以不合适。
    *   简单判断： 如果邻居说“你虽然没当面夸他，但至少心里是佩服他的”，那可能用“At least”。但原文不是这个意思。

3.  C. In fact（事实上）
    *   为什么对？ 这是最合适的！邻居说“Well, no”之后，感觉话没说完，需要给出一个更真实、更具体的解释。“In fact”就像一个“真相开关”，一按下去，后面就要说真正的实际情况了。它把前后两句话连起来，告诉我们：“我不仅没说你坏话，事实上，我还说了你的好话呢！” 非常自然流畅。
    *   简单判断： 当你想要纠正、补充或者强调一个真实情况时，用“In fact”准没错。

4.  D. After all（毕竟）
    *   为什么错？ “After all”表示“毕竟”，用来给出一个理由或原因，有点像中文的“说到底”。比如“别生他气了，他毕竟还是个孩子”。邻居的话并不是在解释“为什么我告诉他这些”的原因，而是在说“我实际上说了什么”。所以这里用“毕竟”说不通。
    *   简单判断： 如果邻居说“我告诉他了，毕竟你真的很欣赏他的店”，那可能会用“After all”。但原文的结构不是这样。

---

背后的知识点（用比喻来说）：

这几个词都是我们说话时的“连接小零件”，它们本身没有实在的意思，

content:
Mr. Johnson, a wise teacher, often tells his students that everyone wants to be respected. In fact, most people are willing to help you if you treat them with respect. This means showing them that their feelings matter and their thoughts are valuable. One day, he shared a story about his neighbor, Mrs. Lee. Mrs. Lee always listened carefully to others and thanked them for their ideas. Because of this, her neighbors were happy to offer help when she needed it. Mr. Johnson hoped his students would learn from this example.

Question: What did Mr. Johnson want his students to learn from Mrs. Lee's story?

options.A: To always ask for help from neighbors
options.B: To show respect to others in daily life
options.C: To share stories with classmates often
options.D: To become a teacher like Mr. Johnson

analysis: 解析：阅读材料中，Mr. Johnson 通过 Mrs. Lee 的例子强调尊重他人的重要性（treat them with respect），并指出这样做能让他人愿意提供帮助。问题询问学生应从故事中学到什么，选项 B '在日常生活中尊重他人' 准确概括了文章的核心主题，其他选项均偏离重点。

content:
Tom is a middle school student who enjoys outdoor activities. Every Saturday morning, he goes to the city park with his friends. They often play basketball, ride bicycles, or simply take walks around the lake. Tom believes that spending time outdoors helps him stay healthy and relaxed. He also likes to observe birds and plants in the park, which makes him feel closer to nature.

What do the first two sentences of the passage mainly tell us?
analysis: 文章前两句为：'Tom is a middle school student who enjoys outdoor activities. Every Saturday morning, he goes to the city park with his friends.' 这两句主要介绍了Tom的身份（中学生）、爱好（户外活动）以及每周六早上的固定活动（去公园），整体描述的是他周末的常规安排，因此选项B（Tom的周末惯例）最符合。选项A只涉及部分细节，C和D是后文延伸的内容，并非前两句的核心。

content:
To help students improve their reading skills, Sunshine Middle School has introduced an online reading platform called "ReadFun". Parents can register on www.readfun.org to follow their children's progress. The registration process is simple. First, visit the website and click the "Sign Up" button. Then, you need to choose your identity: select "Parent" if you are a parent, or "Student" if you are a student. After that, fill in basic information like your name and your child's class. Finally, check your email to confirm the registration. Once registered, parents can view reading reports and get weekly tips.

Question: What should parents do right after clicking the "Sign Up" button on www.readfun.org?
analysis: 根据阅读材料第三句和第四句的描述，注册流程的第一步是点击“Sign Up”按钮，紧接着下一步是选择身份（choose your identity），并明确说明如果是家长应选择“Parent”。因此点击按钮后的直接步骤是选择家长身份，选项B正确。选项A（填写个人信息）发生在选择身份之后；选项C（邮件确认）是最后一步；选项D（查看报告）是注册完成后的功能，均不符合题意。

content:
Reading Fun is a popular website among middle school students. It offers a wide range of reading materials for English learners. The articles are divided into different levels, from easy to difficult, so students can choose what suits them best. Besides articles, the website also has fun videos and games to help improve reading skills. Many teachers recommend this site because it makes learning English enjoyable and effective.

What is the main purpose of the Reading Fun website?

options.B: To help students improve English reading in an enjoyable way.

analysis: 解析：阅读材料明确指出该网站提供不同难度的文章、视频和游戏，旨在让英语学习变得有趣且有效（makes learning English enjoyable and effective）。选项B准确概括了这一主要目的，而其他选项在文中均未提及。

content:
Reading is a wonderful hobby. It can take you to different worlds and times. Many people enjoy reading because it helps them relax and learn new things. However, some books can be difficult to read. They may use complex words or ideas. For these books, it's helpful to have a dictionary nearby. With a dictionary, even challenging books become more accessible.

What does the underlined word "accessible" mean in the passage?
analysis: 根据上下文，前文提到有些书很难读，但使用词典后，即使有挑战性的书也会变得更‘accessible’。这里‘accessible’应与‘difficult to read’形成对比，表示‘容易理解’或‘容易接近’。选项B ‘easy to understand’ 最符合语境。

content:
Reading is a wonderful habit that can open up new worlds for us. According to a recent survey, students who read for at least 30 minutes every day perform better in all subjects. Here are three simple tips to help you become a better reader:

TIP 1: Choose books that interest you. It's easier to stick with reading if you enjoy the topic.
TIP 2: Set aside a regular time for reading each day, such as before bed or after homework.
TIP 3: Share what you read with friends or family. Talking about books makes reading more fun.

Question: According to TIP 2, how often should you set aside time for reading?
analysis: 解析：根据TIP 2的内容“Set aside a regular time for reading each day”，其中明确提到“each day”（每天），因此正确答案是C（每天）。选项A（每周一次）、B（仅在周末）和D（每月两次）均与原文中的“each day”不符。

content:
阅读材料：
Tom is a 14-year-old student who has a special hobby — collecting stamps. He started this hobby three years ago when his grandfather gave him an old album with stamps from different countries. Now Tom has over 500 stamps from more than 40 countries. He keeps them neatly in colored albums and often exchanges duplicates with friends online. Tom says stamps are like tiny windows to the world. Through them, he learns about geography, history, and cultures. His dream is to visit some of the countries whose stamps he collects.

问题：
This passage is mainly written to ____.

options.B: describe a student's hobby and its meaning
options.C: explain how to start collecting stamps

analysis: 解析：全文围绕Tom的集邮爱好展开，介绍了他如何开始、收藏规模、整理方式以及他从中学到的知识，最后提到他的梦想。选项B“描述一名学生的爱好及其意义”全面概括了文章主旨。选项A（宣传邮票展览）、C（解释如何开始集邮）和D（比较青少年的不同爱好）均与文章内容不符。

content:
The school sports meeting is coming next Friday. All classes are busy preparing for it. Class 3A has chosen their players: Tom for the 100-meter race, Lucy for the long jump, and Mike for the relay race. They practice every afternoon after school. Their teacher, Mr. Green, encourages them and gives useful advice. The whole class is working hard to win the first prize. They believe teamwork is very important.

What is the main purpose of the passage?

options.B: To describe how a class prepares for a sports meeting
options.C: To explain the rules of a relay race
options.D: To tell a story about a famous athlete

analysis: 阅读材料主要描述了班级为运动会所做的各项准备，包括选拔运动员、日常训练、老师指导以及班级团队合作等内容，全文围绕“班级筹备运动会”这一主题展开，因此B选项最符合主旨。

content:
Many students find it helpful to use online dictionaries when learning English. These tools provide quick word definitions, example sentences, and even pronunciation guides. However, some teachers worry that relying too much on such digital aids may make students lazy in memorizing vocabulary. They suggest that paper dictionaries, though slower, help learners remember words better because they require more effort to look up.

[问题]
According to the passage, some teachers think using online dictionaries too often is not ______ for students' vocabulary learning.
analysis: 解析：阅读材料中提到“some teachers worry that relying too much on such digital aids may make students lazy in memorizing vocabulary”，并指出纸质词典更能帮助记忆。因此，部分老师认为过度依赖在线词典对词汇学习“没有帮助”，与选项A（helpful）的否定形式对应。其他选项在文中未体现教师的核心批评观点。

content:
In today's digital world, online safety is very important. Many students use the Internet for learning and fun. However, they may face risks like bad information or strangers online. Schools now teach students how to protect themselves online. Teachers play a key role in this education. They not only teach rules but also show students how to follow them.

What should teachers do to help students learn about online safety?

options.A: Avoid using the Internet in class
options.B: Only use old websites for teaching
options.C: Set a good example by following safety rules
options.D: Let students explore the Internet freely

analysis: 阅读材料明确指出教师在网络安全教育中扮演关键角色，不仅要教授规则，还要示范如何遵守（show students how to follow them）。选项C“通过遵守安全规则树立好榜样”与原文中Teachers play a key role...show students how to follow them直接对应，而其他选项要么与原文建议相反（如D），要么未体现教师的示范作用（如A、B）。

content:
Many students choose to buy copied books because they are much cheaper than the original ones. These copied books look almost the same as the copyrighted versions, but their price is usually less than half. However, this behavior is harmful. It not only breaks the law but also hurts the interests of authors and publishers. When authors don't get fair pay for their work, they may lose the motivation to create more good books in the future. Therefore, we should support original books and respect intellectual property.

Question: What is the main problem caused by buying copied books according to the passage?

options.A: They are more expensive than original books.
options.B: They are usually of poor quality.
options.C: They discourage authors from creating new works.
options.D: They are difficult to find in bookstores.

analysis: 文章明确指出，购买盗版书会伤害作者权益，当作者得不到公平报酬时，可能会失去创作更多好书的动力。选项C 'They discourage authors from creating new works'（它们使作者失去创作新作品的积极性）准确概括了这一影响。选项A与事实相反，选项B和D在文中未提及。

content:
Tom is a middle school student who loves basketball. Every day after school, he practices with his friends for an hour. He dreams of becoming a professional basketball player one day. However, his parents worry that too much time on sports might affect his studies. They encourage him to finish homework first. Tom listens to them and now he always completes his schoolwork before playing. He finds that this makes him more focused during practice and he even improves his grades.

Question: How does Tom feel about finishing homework before playing basketball?

options.A: He feels it is a waste of time.
options.B: He finds it helpful for both study and sports.
options.C: He thinks it makes him too tired to play.

analysis: 根据阅读材料最后两句“Tom listens to them and now he always completes his schoolwork before playing. He finds that this makes him more focused during practice and he even improves his grades.”可知，Tom认为先完成作业能让他在练习时更专注，甚至提高了成绩，因此这对他的学习和运动都有帮助。

content:
阅读材料：
Tom is a hardworking student who always finishes his homework on time. However, he sometimes feels tired after long study hours. His teacher, Mr. Lee, noticed this and suggested he take short breaks between tasks. Mr. Lee also adjusted the classroom seating to give Tom more natural light. "A teacher's job," Mr. Lee often says, "is to understand what students truly require to learn better."

问题：
From the story, we can see that Mr. Lee pays great attention to the students' ____.
analysis: 解析：阅读材料中，Mr. Lee 观察到 Tom 学习疲劳后主动建议休息、调整座位，并强调要理解学生“真正需要什么（what students truly require）”。这些行为都体现了对学生需求的关注。选项 C（needs）与原文中“require”及整体情节直接呼应，其他选项（成绩、健康、习惯）虽相关但并非材料强调的核心。

content:
The school library is organizing a reading week. Students are encouraged to borrow books and share their reading experiences. Some choose adventure stories, others prefer science fiction. Everyone is looking forward to the reading sharing session on Friday afternoon.

Which of the following words has a different sound for the underlined part?
analysis: 本题考查元音发音辨析。选项A、B、D中划线部分（oo）均发短音/ʊ/，而选项C中“food”的oo发长音/uː/，发音与其他三项不同。

content:
阅读材料：
Tom and his family moved to a small town last year. They bought an old house near a river. Tom's father spent months repairing it. Now the house looks beautiful with a green garden. Tom planted many flowers there. He often watches birds from his window. However, Tom's father got a new job in another city recently. The family has to move again. Tom feels very sad because he just made friends at school and loves his garden.

问题：
Tom's family will move to another city, but on ______ hand, Tom doesn't want to leave his friends and garden.
analysis: 本题考查固定搭配“on the other hand”（另一方面）。文中提到汤姆一家因父亲工作必须搬家，但汤姆舍不得朋友和花园，前后形成转折关系。该短语中必须使用定冠词the，因此正确答案为C。

content:
Online shopping is now a common way to buy things. It allows people to shop from home at any time. However, there are some risks. We should always check the seller's reputation and read product reviews carefully. Also, it's important to use safe payment methods and protect personal information.

What should we do to stay safe when shopping online?

options.A: Only shop on websites we know well.
options.B: Share our password with family members.

analysis: 阅读材料中提到“check the seller's reputation and read product reviews carefully”以及“use safe payment methods”，这都意味着要在可信的平台上购物。选项A“只在熟悉的网站上购物”符合安全建议。选项B分享密码、选项C只买最便宜的商品、选项D忽略评价都不符合文中提出的安全做法。

content:
阅读材料：
Shirley is a high school student from Shanghai. She is going to visit her cousin in New York City during the summer vacation. Today is her travel day. She arrived at Pudong International Airport two hours early. After checking in her luggage, she went through security and now is sitting in the waiting area. Her flight will depart at 3:00 p.m., but it is only 1:30 p.m. now. Shirley feels a little bored, so she takes out a book and starts reading.

问题：
Where is Shirley waiting for her flight to New York City?
analysis: 解析：阅读材料中明确提到“She arrived at Pudong International Airport”以及“now is sitting in the waiting area”，说明Shirley正在机场候机。因此正确答案是B选项“at the airport”。其他选项与材料内容不符。

content:
Last Friday, our school held a welcome party for some exchange students from Germany. Before the party started, our headmaster, Mr. Chen, gathered all the German visitors in the hall. He wanted to introduce our student volunteers to them first. These volunteers would help the German students during their stay. Mr. Chen said warmly, "Now, let me introduce ______ to you. They are all excellent students and will be your good friends." After the introduction, the party began with laughter and music.

[问题]
Who did the headmaster introduce to the German visitors before the welcome party?
analysis: 本题考查代词宾格的用法。在句子“let me introduce ______ to you”中，动词“introduce”后面需要接宾语，表示“介绍我们”。"we"是主格代词，不能作宾语；"our"是形容词性物主代词，后面需接名词；"ours"是名词性物主代词，表示“我们的（东西）”；只有"us"是宾格代词，在此处作动词"introduce"的宾语，符合语法和语境要求。

content:
The school library is organizing a "Book Review Week" next month. Students are asked to choose a book they like and write a review about it. To write a good review, students need to understand the book's story and characters clearly. They also need to share their own thoughts and feelings about the book. The librarian says that if students can do both well, their reviews will be very helpful to other readers.

What does the librarian think is important for writing a helpful book review?
analysis: 解析：根据阅读材料中图书管理员的话“if students can do both well, their reviews will be very helpful”，明确指出理解书本内容和分享个人感受两者都很重要，且结合使用才能写出有用的书评。因此正确答案是D（两者都需要）。

content:
Last Saturday, our school invited Officer Chen to give a safety talk. He spent nearly an hour explaining how to stay safe online. He told us not to share passwords with friends and to be careful about what we post on social media. He also showed us how to create strong passwords using letters, numbers, and symbols. Everyone listened carefully and learned a lot from his talk.

What did Officer Chen give about online safety?
analysis: 解析：阅读材料中提到警官做了近一小时的讲解（spent nearly an hour explaining），并详细说明了多项安全措施，如不分享密码、谨慎发帖、创建强密码等。这些都属于系统性的“建议”或“指导”，因此“useful advice”（有用的建议）最符合语境。选项B“short note”（简短便条）和D“simple tip”（简单小贴士）都过于简单，无法涵盖近一小时的详细讲解；选项A“long book”（长书）则与讲座形式不符。

content:
Online shopping has become very popular. People can buy things without leaving home. It's convenient but we should also be careful about safety. For example, we should only shop on websites that are safe and well-known. We should never share our passwords with others. Also, it's important to check the product information carefully before buying.

[问题]
According to the passage, what should we do to stay safe when shopping online?

options.A: Buy things from any website we like.
options.C: Shop only on safe and well-known websites.

analysis: 解析：阅读材料中明确提到“we should only shop on websites that are safe and well-known”，这与选项C完全一致。选项A和D与文中建议相反，选项B与文中“never share our passwords”相违背。

content:
阅读材料：
Tom is a middle school student. He likes playing basketball very much. Every day after school, he plays basketball with his friends for one hour. He wants to be a basketball player in the future. His coach tells him that to become a good player, he needs not only practice but also a healthy lifestyle. This includes eating nutritious food, getting enough sleep, and avoiding junk food. Tom follows the advice and starts to eat more vegetables and fruits every day. He also drinks milk regularly to make his bones stronger.

问题：
What does Tom do to make his bones stronger according to the passage?

options.B: He eats more vegetables and fruits.

analysis: 解析：根据阅读材料最后一句“He also drinks milk regularly to make his bones stronger.”可知，Tom通过定期喝牛奶来增强骨骼强度。选项A、B、D虽然也是健康生活方式的一部分，但文中明确提到强健骨骼的直接方法是喝牛奶，因此正确答案是C。

content:
阅读材料：
Tom is a middle school student who is very interested in space exploration. Last night, he watched a documentary about Mars with his father. The program showed how scientists are planning to send humans to the red planet in the future. Tom was so excited that he decided to study harder in science subjects. He hopes to become an astronaut one day and travel to space himself.

问题：
What was Tom doing with his father last night?

options.A: He was reading a book about Mars.
options.B: He was watching a documentary about Mars.
options.C: He was doing his science homework.
options.D: He was talking to a scientist about space.

analysis: 解析：根据阅读材料第二句“Last night, he watched a documentary about Mars with his father.”可知，昨晚汤姆和父亲一起观看了一部关于火星的纪录片。选项B与原文信息完全一致。选项A、C、D的内容在文中均未提及。

content:
The school library is planning to buy new books for students. A survey shows that most students prefer science fiction and adventure stories. The librarian needs to calculate the total cost. Each science fiction book costs ¥35, and each adventure book costs ¥28. The library plans to buy 50 science fiction books and 30 adventure books.

– Can you tell me ______ all these books will be? – Let me calculate it for you.
analysis: 解析：阅读材料中提到图书馆需要计算购买图书的总费用（calculate the total cost），并给出了具体图书的单价和数量。对话中问句的空格后是“all these books will be”，结合上下文，是在询问这些书总共需要多少钱，因此应使用询问价格的“how much”。选项A（how many）询问数量，C（how often）询问频率，D（how long）询问时长，均不符合语境。

content:
阅读材料：
Last weekend, my family and I visited the city science museum. We arrived at 10 a.m. and spent three hours exploring different exhibitions. The space section was my favorite because I saw a real model of a Mars rover. My younger sister enjoyed the dinosaur fossils most. Before we left, we bought some souvenirs at the gift shop. It was an educational and fun day for all of us.

问题：
The writer's family _______ the science museum for three hours last Saturday.
analysis: 解析：阅读材料中明确提到“Last weekend”和“spent three hours”，表明动作发生在过去。因此应使用一般过去时，选项B（visited）为正确形式。选项A为一般现在时，选项C为一般将来时，选项D为现在完成时，均与原文时间状语不符。

content:
The city library is planning a special event for Book Lovers' Week. The event will include book fairs, author talks, and reading corners for children. All activities are free to the public. To make the library more attractive, the main hall ______ with colorful posters and book displays. The library hopes this event will encourage more people to enjoy reading.

Question: What will happen to the main hall of the library for Book Lovers' Week?
analysis: 根据阅读材料中“To make the library more attractive, the main hall ______ with colorful posters...”可知，图书馆大厅将被装饰，这是将来发生的被动动作。主语“the main hall”是动作的承受者，且时间指向未来（为Book Lovers' Week准备），因此应使用将来时的被动语态“will be decorated”。选项A为一般现在时被动，选项B为将来时主动，选项C为现在进行时主动，均不符合语境。

content:
阅读材料：
Vivian is a mother of two children. She believes that childhood should be a time for play and exploration, not just studying. Many parents send their kids to weekend training centers for extra classes, but Vivian thinks it's more important for children to have free time. She wants her children to enjoy outdoor activities, read books they like, and spend time with family. Although some friends advise her to prepare the children for future competitions, Vivian has made her decision.

问题：
What does Vivian refuse to do for her children?

options.A: To send them to weekend training centers.

analysis: 根据阅读材料，Vivian认为童年应该用于玩耍和探索，许多家长送孩子去周末培训班，但她认为给孩子自由时间更重要。文中明确提到她拒绝像其他家长那样送孩子去周末培训班（"Many parents send their kids to weekend training centers for extra classes, but Vivian thinks..."），因此正确答案是A。

content:
阅读材料：
Tom is a middle school student who is very interested in environmental protection. He often joins clean-up activities in his community on weekends. Last Saturday, he and his classmates spent the whole morning picking up plastic bottles and paper in the nearby park. Tom believes that small actions can make a big difference to our planet. He hopes more people will take part in such activities to keep our environment clean.

问题：
Tom and his classmates spent last Saturday morning _______ up waste in the park.
analysis: 本题考查固定搭配“spend time doing sth.”的用法。阅读材料中“spent the whole morning picking up...”提示此处应使用动名词形式。因此正确答案为C选项“picking”。

content:
Tom is visiting Green City Park with his family. He sees many signs in the park. One sign says "NO CYCLING" near the flower garden. Another says "KEEP OFF THE GRASS" on the lawn. Tom also notices a sign with a dog and a red line across it, meaning "NO PETS ALLOWED". His little sister points to a bench under a big tree where an old man is sitting and reading. Next to the bench, there is a small sign that says "QUIET AREA".

[问题]
–______ I ride my bike through the flower garden? –No, you can't. Look at the sign over there.
analysis: 本题考查情态动词在请求许可场景中的用法。对话中询问“我可以在花园里骑车吗？”，回答是“不可以，看那边的标志”。在英语中，表示礼貌地请求许可时常用“May I...?”（我可以……吗？）。选项A（Would）常用于委婉请求，但通常与“you”搭配；C（Must）表示“必须”，与请求许可无关；D（Should）表示“应该”，侧重于建议。根据语境，此处应选择表示请求许可的“May”，故正确答案为B。

content:
阅读材料：
Last weekend, our school organized a tree-planting activity in the city park. More than 100 students took part in it. We worked in small groups and planted over 200 young trees. Although the weather was a little cold, everyone felt warm because we were doing something meaningful for our environment. The park manager thanked us and said these trees would make the air cleaner and the park more beautiful. We all agreed to visit the park regularly to take care of the trees we planted.

问题：
Why did the students feel warm during the activity?

options.D: The park manager gave them hot drinks.

analysis: 解析：根据阅读材料第五句“everyone felt warm because we were doing something meaningful for our environment”可知，学生们感到温暖是因为他们在做有意义的事情。选项B与原文因果关系一致，其他选项在文中均未提及。

content:
Online shopping has become very popular in recent years. Many people enjoy buying things from websites because it saves time and is very convenient. However, there are also some risks. For example, sometimes the product you receive is different from what you saw online. Also, personal information might be stolen if the website is not safe. So, while shopping online, we should choose reliable websites and protect our private information carefully.

According to the passage, online shopping is convenient, but we should also pay attention to ______?
analysis: 解析：阅读材料中提到网上购物虽然方便，但也存在风险，例如收到的商品可能与图片不符、个人信息可能被盗等，因此强调购物时应注意安全问题。问题问的是“但我们也应该注意______”，与材料中“we should also be careful about safety”直接对应，故正确答案为B（安全问题）。

content:
阅读材料：
Lucy is a 14-year-old student who loves painting. She spends every Saturday afternoon at the art club, where she learns watercolor techniques from her teacher, Ms. Green. Lucy dreams of becoming an artist and holding her own exhibition one day. This Saturday, however, her best friend Anna calls her and says, "My family is having a picnic in the park tomorrow. Would you like to join us?"

问题：
What will Lucy most likely reply to Anna?
analysis: 解析：根据阅读材料，Lucy每周六下午都会去美术俱乐部学习水彩画，这是她固定的安排，且她梦想成为艺术家。因此当Anna邀请她周六去野餐时，Lucy很可能会因为已有固定安排而礼貌拒绝。选项A（I'm afraid I can't.）符合语境，既表达了无法参加，又保持了礼貌。其他选项B与她的日程冲突，C和D则不符合Lucy作为朋友应有的礼貌回应。

content:
Many students enjoy keeping pets, but taking care of them requires time and responsibility. Some families choose to adopt pets from animal shelters, while others prefer to buy from pet stores. Each choice has its own reasons.

Adopting a pet can save an animal's life and give it a loving home. Shelters often have many animals waiting for families. ___40___, adoption fees are usually lower than buying from a store. This helps more people afford a pet.

On the other hand, some people want a specific breed of dog or cat. They may choose to buy from a pet store to get exactly what they want. However, this can be more expensive. Also, some worry about where store pets come from.

What is one advantage of adopting a pet from a shelter?

options.A: It allows people to choose rare breeds.
options.B: It is often cheaper than buying from a store.
options.C: It requires less time to take care of the pet.
options.D: It makes sure the pet is always healthy.

analysis: 解析：根据原文第三句“Adoption fees are usually lower than buying from a store.”可知，从收容所领养宠物的费用通常比从宠物店购买更低，这与选项B“它通常比从商店购买更便宜”直接对应。选项A与领养无关，选项C和D在文中未提及。

content: What will you be doing at 8 o'clock tomorrow evening?

options.C: I will be watching a movie.

analysis: 这道题问的是“明天晚上8点你将在做什么？”，强调的是将来某个具体时间点正在进行的动作。将来进行时的结构是“will be + 现在分词”，所以“I will be watching a movie.”（我将在看电影）最符合。A是一般将来时，只表示将来会发生，不强调进行；B是现在进行时，表示现在或计划好的将来安排，但这里时间状语是“tomorrow evening”，用将来进行时更准确；D是一般过去时，时间完全不对。

content: What will you be doing at 8 o'clock tomorrow evening?

options.C: I will be watching a movie.

analysis: 这道题问的是“明天晚上八点你将在做什么？”，强调的是将来某个具体时间点正在进行的动作。将来进行时的结构是“will be + 现在分词”，所以“I will be watching a movie.”（我将在看电影）最符合。A是一般将来时，只表示将来会发生，不强调进行；B是现在进行时，表示现在正在发生或计划好的将来安排，但这里明确有“tomorrow evening”，用将来时更准确；D是一般过去时，表示过去发生，与将来时间矛盾。

content: If I ______ you, I would apologize to her.
analysis: 这道题考的是虚拟语气。在英语中，表示与现在事实相反的假设时，if从句的谓语动词要用过去式（be动词通常用were，不分人称），主句用“would/could/should+动词原形”。这里“If I ______ you”表示“如果我是你（但我实际上不是你）”，是与现在事实相反的假设，所以空里应该填“were”。选项A（am）是现在时，B（was）在口语中有时会用，但正式语法中要求用“were”，D（be）是动词原形，不符合这里的结构。所以正确答案是C。

content: If I ______ you, I would apologize to her.
analysis: 这道题考的是虚拟语气。在英语中，表示与现在事实相反的假设时，if从句的谓语动词要用过去式（be动词通常用were，无论主语是谁），主句用“would/could/should+动词原形”。这里“If I ______ you”表示“如果我是你（但我实际上不是你）”，是与现在事实相反的假设，所以空格处应该填“were”。选项A“am”是一般现在时，B“was”虽然也是过去式，但在规范的虚拟语气中，be动词用“were”更标准，D“be”是动词原形，不符合规则。所以正确答案是C。

content: Look! The light in his room is still on. He ______ at home now.
analysis: 题目说“看！他房间的灯还亮着”，这是一个现在的事实证据。根据这个直接的证据来推测他现在“应该在家”，这是非常有把握的推测，用“must be”（肯定，一定）。其他选项：B “can't be” 表示“不可能在家”，与灯亮着的证据矛盾；C “will be” 表示“将会在家”，是将来时，不符合现在推测的语境；D “should be” 表示“应该在家”，但更多是基于责任或义务的推测，不如“must be”基于证据的推测那么有把握。所以选A。

content: Look! The light in his room is still on. He ______ at home now.
analysis: “must be”表示非常肯定的推测，意思是“一定”，房间里灯还亮着，所以他现在“一定”在家。“can be”表示可能性，但通常用于疑问或否定句。“will be”是将来的意思。“should be”是“应该”，但语气没有“must be”那么肯定。根据“灯亮着”这个明确线索，最确定的推测是A。

content: ______ we need most is clean water and fresh air.
analysis: 这道题考察名词性从句的连接词。句子的主语部分是一个从句，空格处需要填入一个连接词来引导这个主语从句。从句中缺少宾语（need后面缺东西），所以要用能充当成分的what，相当于“the thing that”。that在从句中不充当成分；which表示“哪一个”，需要有选择范围；whether表示“是否”，意思和结构都不合适。整句话意思是“我们最需要的东西是干净的水和新鲜空气。”

content: What will you be doing at 8 o'clock tomorrow evening?

options.C: I will be watching a movie.

analysis: 这道题问的是“明天晚上8点你将在做什么？”，强调的是将来某个具体时间点正在进行的动作。将来进行时的结构是“will be + 现在分词”，所以“I will be watching a movie.”（我将在看电影）是正确的。A选项是一般将来时，只表示将来会发生，不强调进行；B选项是现在进行时，表示现在或近期安排，但这里时间点是“明天”；D选项是一般过去时，与将来时间矛盾。

content: What will you be doing at 8 o'clock tomorrow evening?

options.C: I will be watching a movie.

analysis: 这道题问的是“明天晚上8点你将在做什么？”，强调将来某个具体时间点正在进行的动作。将来进行时的结构是“will be + 现在分词”，所以选项C“I will be watching a movie.”（我将在看电影）是正确的。选项A是一般将来时，只说明天会做，不强调正在进行的动作；选项B是现在进行时，表示现在正在发生；选项D是一般过去时，表示过去发生的事，都不符合题意。

content: The girl ______ is singing on the stage is my cousin.
analysis: 这道题考的是定语从句的关系代词。先行词是“The girl”，指人，并且在从句“______ is singing on the stage”中做主语（正在唱歌的是“她”）。指人且在从句中做主语的，要用“who”或“that”。选项里只有“who”，所以选A。“which”指物；“whom”指人但做宾语；“whose”表示“谁的”或“…的”，表示所有关系，这里都不符合。

content: Look! The light in his room is still on. He ______ at home now.
analysis: 题目说“看！他房间的灯还亮着。他现在______在家。”这是根据眼前灯亮的事实进行肯定推测。must be 表示“一定，肯定”，用于有依据的肯定推测，最符合语境。can be 表示“有时会”，不用于肯定推测；will be 表示“将会”，强调将来；should be 表示“应该”，带有预期或建议的意思，都不符合眼前事实推测的语气。

content: The new library ______ by the mayor last month.
analysis: 句子意思是“新图书馆上个月被市长开放了”。主语是“图书馆”，是“被开放”的对象，所以要用被动语态。时间状语是“last month”（上个月），所以要用一般过去时的被动语态，结构是“was/were + 过去分词”。“library”是单数，所以选“was opened”。其他选项：B是一般现在时的被动，时间不对；C和D是主动语态，不符合“被市长开放”的被动意思。

content: What ________ at eight o'clock tomorrow evening?
analysis: 这道题问的是“明天晚上八点你将在做什么？”，强调的是明天晚上八点那个具体时间点正在进行的动作，所以要用将来进行时（will be doing）。选项A是一般将来时，只表示将来会发生，不强调进行；选项B是现在进行时，可表示近期安排，但不如将来进行时强调具体时刻的进行；选项D是一般现在时，表示习惯或经常性动作，不符合题意。

content: The girl ______ is singing on the stage is my classmate.
analysis: 这道题考的是定语从句的关系代词。先行词是“The girl”（那个女孩），指人，并且在从句“______ is singing on the stage”中做主语（“正在舞台上唱歌”的是她）。指人且在从句中做主语的，要用“who”或“that”，这里选项里有“who”，所以选A。“which”指物；“whom”指人但做宾语；“whose”表示“谁的”或“某物的”，表示所有关系，都不符合这里做主语的语法要求。

content: If I ______ you, I would apologize to her.
analysis: 这道题考的是虚拟语气。在英语中，表示与现在事实相反的假设时，if从句的谓语动词要用过去式（be动词通常用were，无论主语是第几人称）。这里“If I ______ you”表示“如果我是你（但我实际上不是你）”，是与现在事实相反的假设，所以空格处应该填“were”。选项A“am”是一般现在时，用于真实情况；选项B“was”在口语中有时会用，但正式语法中在虚拟语气里应用“were”；选项D“be”是动词原形，不符合这里的语法结构。因此正确答案是C。

content: The girl ______ is singing on the stage is my cousin.
analysis: 这道题考的是定语从句的关系代词。先行词是“The girl”，指人，并且在从句“______ is singing on the stage”中做主语（正在唱歌的是“她”）。指人且在从句中做主语的，要用“who”或“that”。选项B“which”指物；选项C“whom”指人但在从句中做宾语；选项D“whose”表示“谁的”或“…的”，表示所有关系。这里需要主语，所以选A“who”。

content: Look! The lights in Mr. Zhang's office are still on. He ______ be working there now.
analysis: 题目说：“看！张老师办公室的灯还亮着。他现在______还在那里工作。” 这里需要表达一种很有把握的推测。灯亮着是一个很强的证据，表明他很可能在办公室。must 表示“一定，肯定”，用于有把握的肯定推测，最符合语境。A mustn't 表示“禁止”，不用于推测；B can't 表示“不可能”，用于否定推测，意思相反；C might 表示“可能”，语气较弱，不如must有把握。所以选D。

content: By the time we arrived at the cinema, the movie ______ for ten minutes.
analysis: 这道题考的是“过去完成时”。关键信号是“By the time we arrived at the cinema”（当我们到达电影院时），这里的“arrived”是一般过去时，表示一个过去的动作。而电影“开始”这个动作，是发生在我们“到达”这个过去动作之前的，也就是“过去的过去”。在英语中，表示“过去的过去”要用过去完成时（had + 过去分词）。所以这里应该选“had begun”。其他选项：B是一般过去时，只表示过去发生，看不出先后；C是现在完成时，和过去时间无关；D是一般现在时，更不对。

content: The girl ______ is singing on the stage is my sister.
analysis: 这道题考的是定语从句的关系代词。先行词是“The girl”，指人，并且在从句“______ is singing on the stage”中做主语。指人且在从句中做主语的，用“who”或“that”。选项里只有“who”，所以选A。“which”指物；“whom”指人但做宾语；“whose”表示“谁的”或“某物的”，表示所有关系。

content: I don't know ______ we will have the school sports meeting next week. It depends on the weather.
analysis: 解析：句意是“我不知道我们下周是否会开校运会。这取决于天气。”空格后面是一个完整的句子（we will have the school sports meeting next week），不缺主语或宾语，所以不需要连接代词what。根据后半句“这取决于天气”可知，前半句表达的是“是否”开运动会，表示不确定，所以应该用表示“是否”的if来引导宾语从句。that引导从句时表示确定的事实，而when表示时间，都与后半句的语境不符。

content: ______ the heavy rain, they decided to postpone the football match.
analysis: 这道题考查非谓语动词作原因状语。句子主干是“they decided...”，前面需要填入一个表示“因为/看到”的词来引导原因状语。A、B、C都是表示“因为”的介词短语，后面要接名词（the heavy rain），语法上也对，但题目中“the heavy rain”前有空格，如果选它们，句子意思通顺，但通常这类题设计时会更倾向于考查非谓语动词。D选项“Seeing”是现在分词，作状语，意思是“看到/由于看到”，逻辑主语是句子的主语“they”，表示“他们看到大雨，所以决定...”。这是最典型的非谓语动词用法，也是初中重要考点。所以最佳答案是D。

content: If I ______ you, I would apologize to her.
analysis: 这道题考的是虚拟语气，表示与现在事实相反的假设。在虚拟语气中，无论主语是第几人称，be动词的过去式都用"were"。这里"如果我是你"是假设，不是事实，所以选"were"。其他选项：A（am）是一般现在时；B（was）虽然过去式，但虚拟语气中正式用法要求用"were"；D（will be）是一般将来时，都不符合虚拟语气规则。

content: ______ the heavy rain, they decided to postpone the football match.
analysis: 这道题考的是非谓语动词作原因状语。A、B、C三个选项（Because of, Due to, Owing to）都是介词短语，后面要接名词或代词，不能直接接句子。而原句空格后是“the heavy rain”（名词短语），所以从语法上说，A、B、C填入后句子“Because of/Due to/Owing to the heavy rain, they decided...”本身是正确的。但题目是单选题，且四个选项并列，我们需要选出最符合“非谓语动词”考点的选项。D选项“Seeing”是现在分词（非谓语动词的一种），它的逻辑主语是句子的主语“they”，意思是“他们看到大雨”，表主动和原因，相当于“Because they saw the heavy rain”。所以从“考查非谓语动词”的角度，正确答案是D。其他选项是介词，不属于非谓语动词。

content: ______ we need most is clean water and fresh air.
analysis: 这道题考的是名词性从句中连接词的选择。句子主语部分是一个从句，空格处需要填一个连接词来引导这个主语从句。从句中“we need most”缺少宾语（需要某物），所以应该用既能引导从句、又能在从句中充当成分的“What”，相当于“the thing that”。其他选项：B“That”只起连接作用，在从句中不充当成分；C“Which”通常用于有明确范围的选择；D“Whether”表示“是否”，不符合句意。句意为“我们最需要的是干净的水和新鲜的空气”。

content: ________ we need most is clean water and fresh air.
analysis: 这道题考的是名词性从句中连接词的选择。句子主语部分是一个从句，空格处需要填一个连接词来引导这个主语从句。从句中"we need most"缺少宾语，意思是"我们最需要的东西"，所以应该用"What"，它相当于"the thing that"，既能引导从句，又在从句中充当宾语。"That"引导名词性从句时本身无意义，也不在从句中作成分，但这里从句缺宾语，所以不对。"Which"表示"哪一个"，需要有一个明确的选择范围，这里没有。"Whether"表示"是否"，意思不符合。整个句子的意思是"我们最需要的东西是干净的水和新鲜的空气。"

content: By the time we arrived at the cinema, the movie ________ for ten minutes.
analysis: 解析：这道题考的是“过去完成时”，关键是看动作发生的时间顺序。句子里有“By the time we arrived”（在我们到达的时候），这是一个过去的“时间点”。电影开始是在我们到达“之前”就发生了，所以“开始”这个动作发生在“过去的过去”，要用过去完成时（had + 过去分词）。选项A（had begun）和B（had been on）都是过去完成时。但begin是“瞬间动词”，不能和“for ten minutes”（持续十分钟）连用。能表示“持续”状态，并且能和一段时间连用的，是“be on”（上映，进行中）。所以“had been on”既满足了时态要求，又满足了和一段时间连用的逻辑。

content: The new library ______ by the mayor last month.
analysis: 句子的意思是“新图书馆上个月由市长______。” 关键点是“last month”（上个月），表示过去的时间。主语“the new library”是“被打开”的对象，所以要用被动语态。被动语态的结构是“be动词 + 动词过去分词”。这里表示过去，所以be动词用过去式“was”，动词“open”的过去分词是“opened”，因此“was opened”正确。B选项“is opened”是现在时，时间不对。C选项“opened”是主动语态，意思是图书馆自己开了，不合逻辑。D选项“opens”也是主动语态，且是一般现在时，时间和语态都不对。

content: Look! The lights are on. They ______ at home now.
analysis: “must be”表示非常肯定的推测，意思是“一定、肯定”，根据“灯亮着”这个证据，推测他们现在肯定在家。“can be”表示可能性，但不如must肯定，常用于疑问或否定；“will be”表示将来或推测将来；“should be”表示“应该”，带有预期或建议的意思，不如must肯定。所以选A。

content: What will you be doing at 8 o'clock tomorrow evening?

options.B: I will be watching a movie.

analysis: 这道题问的是“明天晚上8点你将在做什么？”，强调在将来某个具体时间点正在进行的动作，应该用将来进行时（will be doing）。选项A是一般将来时，只表示将来会发生，不强调进行；选项C是现在进行时，表示现在正在发生；选项D是过去进行时，表示过去某个时间正在发生。只有B（will be watching a movie）正确表达了“明天晚上8点我将在看电影”这个将来正在进行的动作。

content: I don't know ______ we will have the school sports meeting next week. It depends on the weather.
analysis: 解析：句意是“我不知道我们下周是否会开校运会。这取决于天气。”空格后面是一个完整的句子（we will have...），不缺主语或宾语，所以不需要连接代词what。这里需要表达“是否”的意思，引导宾语从句。if可以引导宾语从句，意为“是否”，符合句意。that在宾语从句中无实义，而这里需要“是否”的含义。when表示“何时”，但后半句说“取决于天气”，强调的是条件而非时间，所以“是否”更合理。

content: The girl ________ is talking to our teacher comes from Canada.
analysis: 这道题考的是定语从句的关系代词选择。先行词是"The girl"，指人，并且在定语从句中作主语（"is talking to our teacher"缺主语）。"who"用于指人，在从句中作主语；"which"指物；"whom"指人但作宾语；"whose"表示所属关系。这里从句缺主语，且指人，所以选A。

content: What ________ at eight o'clock tomorrow evening?
analysis: 这道题问的是“明天晚上八点你将在做什么？”，强调的是明天晚上八点那个具体时间点正在进行的动作，所以要用将来进行时（will be doing）。选项A是一般将来时，只表示将来会发生，不强调进行；选项B是现在进行时，可表示近期安排，但不如将来进行时强调具体时刻的持续性动作；选项D是一般现在时，表示习惯或经常性动作，不符合题意。

content: If I ______ you, I would apologize to her.
analysis: 这道题考的是虚拟语气，表示与现在事实相反的假设。在虚拟语气中，无论主语是第几人称，be动词的过去式都用"were"。这里"如果我是你"是假设，我不是你，所以用"were"。选项A是一般现在时，B是过去式但口语中有时用，不正式，D是将来时，都不符合虚拟语气规则。

content: What will you be doing at 8 o'clock tomorrow evening?

options.B: I will be watching a movie.

analysis: 这道题问的是“明天晚上8点你将在做什么？”，强调的是在将来某个具体时间点正在进行的动作。将来进行时的结构是“will be + 现在分词”，所以“I will be watching a movie.”（我将在看电影）是正确的。A选项是一般将来时，只表示将来会发生，不强调进行；C选项是现在进行时，表示现在正在发生；D选项是一般过去时，表示过去发生的事，都不符合题意。

content: The girl ________ is singing on the stage is my cousin.
analysis: 这道题考的是定语从句的关系代词。先行词是“The girl”，指人，并且在从句“________ is singing on the stage”中做主语（正在唱歌的是“她”）。指人且在从句中做主语的，用“who”或“that”。这里选项中只有“who”符合。“which”指物；“whom”指人但做宾语；“whose”表示“谁的”或“某物的”，表示所有关系。所以正确答案是A。

content: ______ we need most is clean water and fresh air.
analysis: 题目意思是“我们最需要的是干净的水和新鲜的空气。”空格后面是一个完整的句子（we need most），但缺少宾语，且空格处需要引导主语从句并在从句中充当need的宾语。What可以引导名词性从句，并在从句中充当成分（如宾语、主语等），表示“所…的东西/事情”。That引导名词性从句时，在从句中不充当任何成分。Which表示“哪一个”，有选择含义，不符合句意。Why表示原因，也不符合。这里“我们所需要的东西”用What最合适。

content: The problem is ______ we have run out of all the printing paper.
analysis: 这道题问的是“问题是我们已经用完了所有的打印纸”。空格后面“we have run out...”是一个完整的句子（主谓宾齐全），不缺成分，只是对“The problem”进行说明和解释。这种情况下，连接词只起引导作用，不充当从句中的任何成分，所以应该用不充当成分的“that”。what和which在从句中要充当成分（主语、宾语等），whether表示“是否”，不符合句意。因此正确答案是A。

content: By the time we arrived at the cinema, the movie ______ for ten minutes.
analysis: 这道题考的是过去完成时。关键信号是“By the time we arrived at the cinema”（当我们到达电影院的时候），这里的“arrived”是一般过去时，表示一个过去的动作。而电影“开始”这个动作，发生在我们“到达”这个过去动作之前，也就是“过去的过去”。对于“过去的过去”发生的动作，我们要用过去完成时（had + 过去分词）。所以这里应该选“had begun”。其他选项：B是一般过去时，只表示过去发生，看不出先后；C是现在完成时，和过去时间无关；D是一般现在时，更不对。

content: By the time we arrived at the cinema, the movie ________ for ten minutes.
analysis: 解析：这道题的关键是“for ten minutes”（持续了十分钟），它表示一个持续的状态。过去完成时“had done”表示“过去的过去”。A选项“had begun”（已经开始）是瞬间动词，不能和“for+一段时间”连用。B选项“had been on”（已经上映）中的“be on”表示“上映、进行中”的状态，可以持续，所以正确。C和D选项“began”和“was on”都是一般过去时，而句中有“By the time we arrived”（在我们到达的时候），主句动作发生在“到达”之前，所以必须用过去完成时，不能用一般过去时。

content: The new library ________ by the mayor last month.
analysis: 句子的主语是“新图书馆”，它是“被开放”的对象，所以要用被动语态。时间状语是“上个月”，表示过去，所以要用一般过去时的被动语态，结构是“was/were + 过去分词”。“open”的过去分词是“opened”，主语是单数，所以选“was opened”。其他选项：A是一般现在时的被动；C是主动语态，意思是图书馆自己开了；D是现在完成时的主动语态，都不符合“上个月被开放”的被动和过去时态。

content: ______ to improve her spoken English, Lucy practices with foreigners every weekend.
analysis: 解析：句子主语是Lucy，空格处需要表示“为了”或“怀着…的目的”的非谓语动词来作状语。这里“提高英语口语”是Lucy主动、有意识的目的，应该用现在分词“Hoping”表主动和伴随状态。A“Hoped”过去分词表被动，逻辑不对；B“To hope”不定式常表目的，但“To hope”本身意思是“为了希望”，后面接“to improve”会显得重复别扭；D“Hope”是动词原形，不能直接这样用。所以选C，意思是“怀着提高英语口语的希望，Lucy每周末都和外国人练习”。

content: If I ______ you, I would apologize to her.
analysis: 这道题考的是虚拟语气中与现在事实相反的假设。句意是“如果我是你，我会向她道歉。”但“我是你”不是真的，只是一种假设。在虚拟语气中，无论主语是I, he, she还是it，be动词一律用“were”，不能用was或am。所以选C。

content: ______ the heavy rain, they decided to postpone the football match.
analysis: 这道题考的是非谓语动词作原因状语。A、B、C三个选项（Because of, Due to, Owing to）都是介词短语，后面要接名词或代词，但空格后面是“the heavy rain”，这是一个名词短语，所以从语法上说A、B、C都正确。但题目是单选题，只有一个最佳答案。这里“Seeing”是现在分词（非谓语动词的一种），意思是“考虑到、鉴于”，逻辑主语是句子的主语“they”，表示“他们看到/考虑到大雨”，所以决定推迟比赛。用现在分词短语作原因状语更符合英语的常见表达，也是初中阶段强调的一个重点。所以D是最佳答案。

content: What ______ you ______ at this time tomorrow?
analysis: 这道题问的是“明天这个时候你将在做什么？”，强调的是明天某个具体时间点正在进行的动作，所以要用将来进行时（will be doing）。选项A是一般将来时，只表示将来会发生，不强调进行；选项B是现在进行时，表示现在正在做；选项D是一般现在时，表示习惯或经常性动作。只有C（will be doing）符合将来进行时的结构。

content: The new library ______ by the mayor last month.
analysis: 句子的意思是“新图书馆上个月由市长______。” 关键词是“by the mayor”（被市长）和“last month”（上个月）。有“by+人”通常表示被动，即“图书馆被开放”。时间“last month”是过去，所以要用过去时的被动语态，结构是“was/were + 过去分词”。“open”的过去分词是“opened”，主语“library”是单数，所以选“was opened”。其他选项：B是一般现在时的被动，时间不对；C是主动语态，但主语是物，一般不会自己“开放”；D是一般现在时主动语态，不符合句意和语法。

content: By the time we arrived at the cinema, the movie ________ for ten minutes.
analysis: 解析：这道题考的是“过去完成时”，并且要选一个能表示“持续”状态的词。句子的意思是“当我们到达电影院时，电影已经开演十分钟了。” “By the time we arrived” 表示“在我们到达的那个时间点之前”，所以“开演”这个动作发生在“到达”之前，是“过去的过去”，要用过去完成时（had + 过去分词）。选项A “had begun” 是“已经开始”，但“begin”是瞬间动词，不能和“for ten minutes”这样表示一段时间的短语连用。选项B “had been on” 中，“be on”表示（电影、演出等）“在上演、在进行”，是表示状态的短语，可以延续，所以能和“for ten minutes”搭配，意思是“已经上演了十分钟”。选项C “began”是一般过去时，时态不对。选项D “has begun”是现在完成时，时态也不对。因此正确答案是B。

content: By the time we arrived at the cinema, the movie ______ for ten minutes.
analysis: 这道题考的是“过去完成时”。关键信号是“By the time we arrived at the cinema”（当我们到达电影院的时候），这里的“arrived”是一般过去时。电影开始（begin）这个动作，发生在我们“到达”这个过去动作之前，也就是“过去的过去”。对于“过去的过去”发生的动作，我们要用“过去完成时”（had + 过去分词）。所以这里应该选“had begun”。其他选项：B“began”是一般过去时，只表示过去发生，但体现不出“在我们到达之前”；C“has begun”是现在完成时，和过去的时间点“arrived”对不上；D“begins”是一般现在时，更不对。

content: Look! The light in his room is still on. He ______ be at home now.
analysis: 题目说“看！他房间的灯还亮着”，这是一个现在的事实证据。根据强证据进行肯定推测“他现在一定在家”时，要用must。A mustn't表示“禁止”，不用于推测；B can't用于否定推测“不可能”；C might表示“可能”，但语气较弱，不如must有说服力。

content: If I ______ you, I would apologize to her.
analysis: 这道题考的是虚拟语气。在英语中，表示与现在事实相反的假设时，if从句的谓语动词要用过去式（be动词通常用were），主句用“would/could/should+动词原形”。这里“If I ______ you”表示“如果我是你（但我实际上不是你）”，是与现在事实相反，所以空格处应该填“were”。选项A“am”是现在时，B“was”在口语中有时可用，但正式语法中要求用“were”，D“be”是原形，都不符合虚拟语气结构。

content: ______ the heavy rain, they decided to stay at home.
analysis: 句子意思是“由于看到大雨，他们决定待在家里”。空格处需要填一个非谓语动词来引导原因状语。主语“they”和“看”这个动作是主动关系，并且“看”这个动作发生在“决定”之前，表示原因。现在分词“Seeing”可以表示主动和原因，符合题意。B项“Seen”是被动，C项“To see”通常表目的或将来，D项“See”是动词原形，不能直接这样用。所以选A。

content:
阅读材料: At Maplewood Middle School, a unique tradition called "Silent Day" takes place every semester. On this day, students and teachers communicate solely through written notes, gestures, or digital messages—no spoken words are allowed. Initially designed to promote empathy for hearing-impaired peers, the event has evolved into a profound lesson in communication. Students report paying closer attention to body language and facial expressions. Some even discover that their quieter classmates become more expressive through writing or art. However, critics argue that it disrupts normal learning, especially in subjects like foreign language or music. Despite this, the school insists the benefits in fostering patience and alternative communication skills outweigh the drawbacks.

问题: What is the primary purpose of the "Silent Day" tradition according to the passage?

options.A: To improve students' foreign language pronunciation
options.B: To help students understand the challenges faced by hearing-impaired people
options.C: To reduce noise pollution in the school environment
options.D: To encourage students to use digital devices more frequently

analysis: 解析: 文章明确提到该传统最初是为了『promote empathy for hearing-impaired peers』（增进对听力障碍同伴的同理心），这与选项B的表述一致。选项A和D是文中提到的次要细节或误解；选项C在文中未被提及。本题需要学生准确识别事件的核心目的，而非次要影响或片面信息。

content:
阅读材料：
When Marie Curie was a student in Paris, she lived in a tiny attic with almost no heating. To save money, she often skipped meals and survived on bread and tea. During winter, she would wear all her clothes to bed to keep warm. Despite these hardships, her passion for science never faded. She spent days and nights in the laboratory, conducting experiments that would later lead to the discovery of radium. In 1903, she became the first woman to win a Nobel Prize, and in 1911, she won a second Nobel Prize in chemistry—the only person to achieve this in two different scientific fields.

问题：
What is the main reason the author mentions Marie Curie's difficult living conditions in the passage?

options.A: To criticize the poor support for female scientists at that time
options.B: To highlight how her determination led to great achievements
options.C: To explain why she moved from Poland to Paris
options.D: To show that poverty is necessary for scientific success

analysis: 文章通过描述居里夫人艰苦的生活条件（如挨饿、受冻），与她后来取得两次诺贝尔奖的成就形成对比，旨在强调她的毅力和热情如何克服困难并取得巨大科学突破。选项A虽涉及历史背景，但并非作者主要意图；选项C在文中未提及原因；选项D的表述绝对化且不符合文章主旨。

content:
阅读材料：
While many people focus on avoiding sugar and fat, the concept of 'nutrient density' offers a more nuanced approach to healthy eating. Nutrient-dense foods provide substantial amounts of vitamins, minerals, and other beneficial compounds relative to their calorie content. For example, leafy greens, berries, and legumes are highly nutrient-dense, whereas processed snacks are 'energy-dense' but 'nutrient-poor.' Interestingly, some foods like nuts and avocados are both energy-dense and nutrient-dense, meaning they are calorie-rich but also packed with essential nutrients. The key is balancing these within your overall diet rather than eliminating any single category.

问题：According to the passage, what is the main difference between 'nutrient-dense' foods and 'energy-dense' foods in terms of dietary value?

options.A: Nutrient-dense foods are always low in calories, while energy-dense foods are high in calories.
options.B: Nutrient-dense foods provide more essential nutrients per calorie, while energy-dense foods may lack nutrients despite having calories.
options.C: Energy-dense foods are unhealthy and should be completely avoided, unlike nutrient-dense foods.
options.D: Nutrient-dense foods contain only vitamins, while energy-dense foods contain only fats and sugars.

analysis: 解析：正确答案是B。文章明确指出，营养密集型食物（nutrient-dense foods）相对于其卡路里含量能提供大量维生素、矿物质等有益成分，而加工零食等能量密集型食物（energy-dense foods）则可能'营养贫乏'（nutrient-poor）。选项A错误，因为文中举例坚果和牛油果既是能量密集型也是营养密集型，说明营养密集型食物不一定低卡。选项C与文中'关键在于平衡而非完全消除'的观点矛盾。选项D过于绝对化，与文章描述不符。

content:
Tom is a 13-year-old boy. His favorite hobby is reading. Every day after school, he spends one hour reading books in his room. He likes storybooks best, especially adventure stories. Tom also enjoys sharing his favorite stories with his friends. Sometimes, he even writes short stories himself. Tom thinks reading helps him learn new words and understand the world better. He believes that reading is not only fun but also useful.

Question: What is Tom's favorite hobby?
analysis: 文章第一句明确提到'His favorite hobby is reading'，第二句进一步说明他每天花一小时读书，因此正确答案是C。其他选项在文中均未提及。

content:
Many teenagers enjoy playing video games, but for 14-year-old Leo, his true passion lies in something quieter and more creative: building model ships. He spends hours carefully gluing tiny wooden pieces together and painting them with fine brushes. His room is filled with completed models, from ancient sailing ships to modern warships. Leo says the process helps him relax and improves his concentration. He also learns about history and engineering through researching different ship designs. His parents support his hobby because they see how it develops his patience and attention to detail.

Question: What is the main reason Leo's parents support his hobby?

options.A: It helps him make new friends.
options.B: It improves his concentration and patience.
options.C: It teaches him how to play video games better.
options.D: It allows him to travel to different countries.

analysis: 文章最后一句明确指出，Leo的父母支持他的爱好是因为他们看到这培养了他的耐心和注意力（'develops his patience and attention to detail'）。选项B与此完全对应。选项A、C、D在文中均未提及或与内容不符。

content:
The Dragon Boat Festival is a traditional Chinese festival. It falls on the fifth day of the fifth month in the Chinese lunar calendar. People celebrate it to remember Qu Yuan, a great poet and patriot in ancient China. On this day, families often eat zongzi, which is a kind of sticky rice dumpling wrapped in bamboo leaves. People also watch or take part in dragon boat races. These races are exciting and full of team spirit. The festival is not only popular in China but also in many other countries.

Question: Why do people celebrate the Dragon Boat Festival?

options.B: To remember the poet Qu Yuan.

analysis: 文章第二句明确指出：“People celebrate it to remember Qu Yuan, a great poet and patriot in ancient China.” 因此，庆祝端午节是为了纪念诗人屈原。选项A、C、D在文中均未提及。

content:
The Dragon Boat Festival, also known as Duanwu Festival, is a traditional Chinese holiday celebrated on the fifth day of the fifth lunar month. It honors the famous poet Qu Yuan, who lived over 2,000 years ago. People celebrate by eating zongzi, which are sticky rice dumplings wrapped in bamboo leaves, and by holding dragon boat races. The races are exciting team competitions where long, narrow boats decorated like dragons are paddled to the beat of drums. This festival is not only fun but also a way for people to remember Chinese history and culture.

Question: What is the main purpose of the Dragon Boat Festival according to the passage?

options.A: To enjoy delicious food and watch exciting races.
options.B: To honor the ancient poet Qu Yuan and remember Chinese culture.
options.C: To celebrate the beginning of summer every year.
options.D: To compete in international dragon boat competitions.

analysis: 文章明确指出端午节是为了纪念诗人屈原（honors the famous poet Qu Yuan），并且是让人们铭记中国历史和文化的方式（a way for people to remember Chinese history and culture）。选项B准确概括了这一主要目的。选项A描述了节日的活动，但不是主要目的；选项C和D在文中未提及或不是核心信息。

content:
阅读材料：When Thomas Edison was a boy, his teacher told his mother he was 'too stupid to learn anything.' Edison's mother decided to teach him at home. He developed hearing problems at age 12, which some believe helped him concentrate better. Edison's first patent was for an electric vote recorder, but politicians weren't interested. He didn't give up. Later, he invented the phonograph and improved the light bulb. Edison held 1,093 patents, but his greatest invention might be the industrial research lab, where teams worked together systematically. He believed genius is 'one percent inspiration and ninety-nine percent perspiration.'

问题：According to the passage, what is considered Edison's most significant contribution?

options.A: The invention of the practical light bulb
options.B: The creation of the first phonograph
options.C: The establishment of the industrial research lab model

analysis: 解析：文章明确提到'but his greatest invention might be the industrial research lab'，指出爱迪生最重要的发明可能是工业研究实验室模式。其他选项（电灯泡、留声机、专利数量）虽是他的成就，但文章通过'might be'的强调句式将工业研究实验室定位为最具意义的贡献。此题考查对文章细节和评价性语句的精准理解。

content:
The Dragon Boat Festival, also known as Duanwu Festival, is a traditional Chinese holiday celebrated on the fifth day of the fifth lunar month. It honors the famous poet Qu Yuan, who lived during the Warring States period. People celebrate by eating zongzi, which are sticky rice dumplings wrapped in bamboo leaves, and by holding dragon boat races. The festival is not only a time for fun but also a way to remember Chinese history and culture. Many families hang special herbs on their doors to protect against illness, following ancient customs.

Question: What is the main purpose of celebrating the Dragon Boat Festival according to the passage?

options.A: To enjoy delicious food and exciting races only.
options.B: To remember and honor the poet Qu Yuan and Chinese traditions.
options.C: To protect families from illness with herbs.
options.D: To mark the beginning of the summer season.

analysis: 文章明确指出端午节是为了纪念诗人屈原和中国历史文化（"honors the famous poet Qu Yuan" 和 "a way to remember Chinese history and culture"）。选项A片面（"only"错误），选项C和D是节日的部分习俗或关联，但不是主要目的。

content:
When Thomas Edison was a child, his teacher told his mother that he was "too stupid to learn anything." Edison's mother decided to teach him at home. She encouraged his curiosity and love for experiments. Edison later said, "My mother was the making of me.
