She was so true, so sure of me, and I felt I had someone to live for, someone I must not disappoint." This early support helped him become one of the greatest inventors in history, holding over 1,000 patents.

Question: Why did Edison's mother decide to teach him at home?

options.A: Because the school asked her to do so.
options.B: Because his teacher thought Edison couldn't learn well at school.
options.C: Because Edison was too young for school.
options.D: Because she was a teacher herself.

analysis: 根据文章第一句，老师告诉爱迪生的母亲他“太笨了，学不会任何东西”，母亲因此决定在家教他。选项B准确对应了这一原因。选项A、C、D在文中均未提及或与事实不符。

content:
The Great Pacific Garbage Patch is a huge area of floating plastic waste in the Pacific Ocean. It is formed by ocean currents that collect trash from coasts and ships. Most of the waste is plastic, which does not break down easily. Instead, it breaks into tiny pieces called microplastics. These microplastics are eaten by sea animals and can enter the food chain, harming both animals and humans.

Scientists and volunteers are working to clean up the garbage patch. They use special nets to collect the plastic. However, cleaning the entire area is very difficult and expensive. The best solution is to reduce plastic use and stop waste from entering the ocean in the first place.

Question: What is the main reason why the Great Pacific Garbage Patch is harmful?

options.A: It makes the ocean water dirty and changes its color.
options.B: It collects plastic that breaks into microplastics and enters the food chain.
options.C: It is very expensive and difficult for ships to travel through.
options.D: It prevents ocean currents from moving normally.

analysis: 文章明确指出，塑料垃圾会分解成微塑料，被海洋动物吞食并进入食物链，对动物和人类造成伤害。选项B准确概括了这一核心危害。选项A、C、D在文中未作为主要危害被强调，或属于次要信息。

content:
Thomas Edison was a great inventor. He invented many things, like the light bulb. When he was a child, he liked to ask questions. His teacher thought he asked too many questions. So his mother taught him at home. Edison worked very hard. He tried thousands of times to make the light bulb work. He never gave up. He said, "I have not failed. I've just found 10,000 ways that won't work." His story teaches us that hard work and not giving up are important.

Question: What did Edison's mother do when his teacher thought he asked too many questions?

options.A: She asked him to stop asking questions.
options.C: She sent him to another school.

analysis: 根据文章第三句和第四句：'His teacher thought he asked too many questions. So his mother taught him at home.' 可知，当老师认为爱迪生问题太多时，他的母亲决定在家教他。因此正确答案是B。

content:
Thomas Edison was a famous American inventor. He is best known for inventing the light bulb. When he was a child, he liked to ask questions and try new things. He did not do well in school, but his mother taught him at home. Edison failed many times before he made the first working light bulb. He once said, "I have not failed. I've just found 10,000 ways that won't work." His story teaches us that hard work and never giving up are important.

Question: What did Edison's mother do when he didn't do well in school?

options.B: She sent him to another school.
options.C: She asked him to stop studying.
options.D: She helped him invent the light bulb.

analysis: 根据文章第三句“He did not do well in school, but his mother taught him at home.”可知，当爱迪生在学校表现不好时，他的母亲在家教他。因此正确答案是A。选项B、C、D在文中均未提及。

content:
阅读材料: The arctic fox is a small mammal that lives in the cold Arctic regions. Its most amazing feature is its fur, which changes color with the seasons. In winter, its coat turns pure white to blend in with the snow, helping it hide from predators and sneak up on prey like lemmings. In summer, its fur changes to brown or gray, matching the rocks and plants of the tundra. This color change is controlled by daylight hours. As days grow shorter in autumn, a hormone triggers the growth of white fur. Arctic foxes also have thick fur on their paws, acting like natural snow boots to keep their feet warm and help them walk on ice.

问题: What is the main reason the arctic fox's fur changes color?

options.A: To attract a mate during different seasons
options.B: To match the changing environment for survival
options.C: To show its age and health to other foxes
options.D: To absorb different amounts of sunlight for warmth

analysis: 解析: 文章明确指出，北极狐的皮毛随季节变色主要是为了融入环境（blend in with the snow / matching the rocks and plants），从而帮助它躲避天敌和捕猎，这是为了生存的适应性特征。选项A（吸引配偶）、C（展示年龄和健康）和D（吸收阳光保暖）在文中均未提及。

content:
Dolphins are very smart animals. They live in the sea and like to play. Dolphins often swim in groups called pods. They talk to each other with clicks and whistles. Dolphins are also friendly to people. Sometimes they help fishermen find fish. They can even jump high out of the water. Many people love to watch dolphins because they are so clever and fun.

Question: What do dolphins use to talk to each other?
analysis: 答案在原文第四句直接给出：'They talk to each other with clicks and whistles.'（它们用咔哒声和口哨声交流）。其他选项在文中均未提及。

content:
阅读材料：
Have you ever wondered why some animals can see in the dark while humans cannot? The secret lies in a special layer behind their retinas called the tapetum lucidum. This layer acts like a mirror, reflecting light that passes through the retina back onto the light-sensitive cells. This gives the cells a second chance to detect the light, significantly improving night vision. Animals like cats, dogs, and deer possess this feature, which is why their eyes seem to glow when light shines on them in the dark. Humans lack this reflective layer, so we rely more on artificial light to see clearly at night.

问题：
What is the primary function of the tapetum lucidum in animals' eyes?

options.B: To reflect light back onto the retina for better night vision.
options.C: To protect the eyes from bright sunlight.
options.D: To change eye color in different lighting conditions.

analysis: 解析：文章明确指出，tapetum lucidum 的作用是像镜子一样反射穿过视网膜的光线，让感光细胞有第二次机会探测光线，从而显著改善夜视能力（...reflecting light that passes through the retina back onto the light-sensitive cells... improving night vision）。选项B准确概括了这一功能。选项A（产生更多感光细胞）和C（保护眼睛免受强光伤害）在文中未提及；选项D（改变眼睛颜色）是对眼睛发光现象的误解，并非该结构的主要功能。

content:
The concept of 'digital twins' is revolutionizing industries from manufacturing to urban planning. A digital twin is a virtual, dynamic replica of a physical object, system, or process. It is created using real-time data from sensors on the physical counterpart. This allows engineers and planners to simulate, predict, and optimize performance in the virtual world before making changes in reality. For instance, a city can create a digital twin of its traffic network. By running simulations, planners can test the impact of a new bridge or a changed bus route on traffic flow and pollution levels without disrupting actual traffic. The core value lies in its predictive power and risk-free experimentation environment.

Question: What is the primary purpose of creating a digital twin according to the passage?

options.A: To replace physical objects with virtual models entirely.
options.B: To collect and store large amounts of real-time sensor data.
options.C: To test and optimize changes safely in a virtual environment before real-world application.
options.D: To reduce the cost of manufacturing physical prototypes.

analysis: 解析：文章明确指出数字孪生的核心价值在于其预测能力和无风险的实验环境（'The core value lies in its predictive power and risk-free experimentation environment.'）。前文举例也说明，可以在虚拟世界中模拟、预测和优化性能，然后再在现实中实施改变（'to simulate, predict, and optimize performance in the virtual world before making changes in reality'）。选项C准确概括了这一主要目的。选项A（完全用虚拟模型替代物理对象）文章未提及且过于绝对；选项B（收集存储数据）是创建过程的一部分，而非根本目的；选项D（降低制造成本）可能是其带来的好处之一，但并非文章阐述的核心目的。

content:
阅读材料: The concept of 'digital twins' is revolutionizing manufacturing and urban planning. A digital twin is a virtual, dynamic replica of a physical object or system, such as a jet engine, a factory, or even an entire city. It is created using real-time data from sensors on the physical counterpart. This allows engineers and planners to simulate scenarios, predict failures, and optimize performance without interfering with the real-world object. For instance, a city's digital twin can model traffic flow under different conditions or the impact of a new building on wind patterns. The core value lies not just in mirroring reality, but in enabling predictive analysis and informed decision-making before actions are taken in the physical world.

问题: What is the primary purpose of creating a digital twin according to the passage?

options.A: To create an exact and static copy of a physical object for display.
options.B: To replace physical objects with virtual models entirely.
options.C: To enable predictive analysis and informed decision-making.
options.D: To collect sensor data without any further application.

analysis: 解析: 文章明确指出数字孪生的核心价值（The core value lies...）在于实现预测性分析和在现实世界行动前做出明智决策（enabling predictive analysis and informed decision-making）。选项A错误，因为数字孪生是动态的（dynamic），而非静态的（static）。选项B错误，文章未提及完全替代物理对象。选项D错误，因为收集数据是为了进一步的应用，如模拟和优化。

content:
Last summer, my family went to Sanya for a holiday. Sanya is a beautiful city in Hainan, China. It is famous for its blue sea and white beaches. We stayed there for five days. Every morning, we walked on the beach and swam in the warm sea. We also ate delicious seafood. My favorite activity was building sandcastles with my little sister. We took many photos. I really enjoyed this trip and hope to visit Sanya again.

Question: What was the writer's favorite activity during the trip?
analysis: 文章明确提到'My favorite activity was building sandcastles with my little sister.'，因此作者最喜欢的活动是堆沙堡。其他选项虽在文中出现，但并非作者明确指出的'favorite activity'。

content:
The concept of 'digital twins' is revolutionizing manufacturing and urban planning. A digital twin is a virtual, real-time replica of a physical object or system, such as a jet engine, a building, or even an entire city. It is created using data from sensors installed on the physical counterpart. This data allows the digital twin to simulate, predict, and optimize the performance of its physical twin. For instance, engineers can test stress scenarios on a digital model of a bridge without any real-world risk. The core value lies not just in mirroring reality, but in using the virtual model to inform decisions and prevent problems before they occur in the physical world.

Question: What is the primary purpose of creating a digital twin according to the passage?

options.A: To create an exact visual copy for display purposes.
options.B: To replace physical objects entirely with virtual models.
options.C: To simulate and optimize performance to prevent real-world problems.
options.D: To collect sensor data for academic research only.

analysis: 解析：文章明确指出数字孪生的核心价值在于利用虚拟模型进行模拟、预测和优化，从而在实际物理问题发生前做出决策和预防（...using the virtual model to inform decisions and prevent problems before they occur...）。选项A提到的'展示用途'、选项B的'完全替代'以及选项D的'仅用于学术研究'均与文中描述的主要目的不符。

content:
The Dragon Boat Festival, also known as Duanwu Festival, is a traditional Chinese holiday celebrated on the fifth day of the fifth lunar month. It honors the famous poet Qu Yuan, who lived during the Warring States period. People celebrate by eating zongzi, which are sticky rice dumplings wrapped in bamboo leaves, and by holding dragon boat races. The festival is not only a time for fun but also a way to remember Chinese history and culture. Many families hang special herbs on their doors to protect against illness, following ancient customs.

Question: What is the main purpose of celebrating the Dragon Boat Festival according to the passage?

options.A: To enjoy delicious food and exciting races only.
options.B: To remember and honor the poet Qu Yuan and Chinese traditions.
options.C: To protect families from illness with herbs.
options.D: To mark the beginning of the summer season.

analysis: 文章明确指出端午节是为了纪念诗人屈原和中国历史文化（"honors the famous poet Qu Yuan" 和 "a way to remember Chinese history and culture"）。选项A片面（"only"错误），选项C和D是节日的部分习俗或关联，但不是主要目的。

content:
阅读材料：
The concept of 'slow travel' is gaining popularity as an alternative to traditional tourism. Instead of rushing to visit as many landmarks as possible, slow travelers immerse themselves in the local culture of a single destination. They might rent an apartment for a month, shop at neighborhood markets, and learn basic phrases in the local language. This approach emphasizes meaningful connections over checklist sightseeing. Proponents argue that slow travel reduces environmental impact and supports local economies more sustainably. However, critics note that it requires more time and flexibility, which may not be feasible for everyone.

问题：
According to the passage, what is the main difference between slow travel and traditional tourism?

options.A: Slow travel focuses on visiting more famous landmarks.
options.B: Slow travel prioritizes cultural immersion over quantity of sights.
options.C: Slow travel is cheaper and requires less planning.
options.D: Slow travel appeals mainly to tourists with limited vacation time.

analysis: 解析：文章明确指出，慢旅行与传统旅游的核心区别在于：传统旅游追求尽可能多地参观地标（'rushing to visit as many landmarks as possible'），而慢旅行则强调深入体验当地文化（'immerse themselves in the local culture'）。选项B准确概括了这一对比。选项A与原文相反；选项C中的'cheaper'和'less planning'未提及；选项D与原文'requires more time'矛盾。

content:
Traveling to a new country often involves more than just sightseeing; it requires cultural sensitivity. In Japan, for instance, it's considered impolite to eat or drink while walking in public. In many Middle Eastern countries, showing the soles of your feet is offensive. However, some rules are less obvious. In Thailand, touching someone's head, even a child's, is disrespectful as the head is viewed as the most sacred part of the body. Conversely, pointing with your feet is rude in several Asian cultures. Understanding these nuances not only shows respect but also enriches your travel experience by fostering genuine connections with locals.

Question: According to the passage, which of the following behaviors is considered disrespectful in Thailand?

options.B: Showing the soles of your feet.

analysis: 解析：文章明确提到在泰国，触摸他人的头部（即使是小孩的）被视为不尊重，因为头部被认为是身体最神圣的部位。选项A是日本的文化禁忌；选项B是许多中东国家的习俗；选项D在文中未提及为不礼貌行为。因此正确答案为C。

content:
Many people think recycling is only about plastic bottles and paper, but electronic waste, or e-waste, is becoming a bigger problem. Old phones, computers, and batteries contain harmful materials. If thrown into regular trash, these materials can pollute soil and water. However, e-waste also has valuable metals like gold and copper. Recycling them properly not only protects the environment but also saves resources. Some companies now offer e-waste recycling programs. You can bring your old devices to special collection points instead of throwing them away.

Question: What is the main idea of the passage?

options.A: Plastic recycling is the most important for the environment.
options.B: E-waste is harmful but can be recycled to protect the environment and save resources.
options.C: All waste should be thrown into regular trash bins.
options.D: Only companies should deal with electronic devices.

analysis: 文章主要讨论了电子垃圾的危害性（含有害物质、污染环境）以及正确回收的益处（保护环境、节约资源），并提到了回收途径。选项B完整概括了这一核心内容。A项偏离主题（文章重点不是塑料回收），C项与文章建议相反，D项过于绝对且与个人可参与回收的描述不符。

content:
Healthy eating is not about strict diets, but about balance and variety. A balanced diet includes foods from all five main groups: fruits, vegetables, grains, protein foods, and dairy. For example, you can start your day with whole-grain bread and a glass of milk, have chicken with vegetables for lunch, and enjoy some fruit as an afternoon snack. It's also important to drink plenty of water and limit foods high in sugar, salt, or unhealthy fats. Remember, occasional treats are fine, but the key is to make healthy choices most of the time. This way, you give your body the energy and nutrients it needs to grow and stay active.

Question: According to the passage, what is the main idea of healthy eating?

options.A: Following a very strict diet with no treats.
options.B: Eating only fruits and vegetables every day.
options.C: Balancing different food groups and making healthy choices regularly.
options.D: Drinking a lot of water and avoiding all fats.

analysis: 文章开篇明确指出健康饮食不是严格节食，而是关于平衡与多样化（balance and variety），并详细说明了包含五大类食物、多喝水、限制不健康食物等建议，最后总结关键在于大多数时间做出健康选择。选项C准确概括了这一核心观点。A与文中'occasional treats are fine'矛盾；B过于片面，忽略了其他食物类别；D中的'avoiding all fats'表述绝对，文中只建议限制不健康脂肪。

content:
阅读材料: In recent years, artificial intelligence (AI) has become a part of our daily lives. From voice assistants like Siri to recommendation systems on shopping websites, AI helps us in many ways. However, some people worry that AI might take over human jobs in the future. Experts say that while AI can do repetitive tasks faster, it still lacks human creativity and emotional understanding. Therefore, instead of replacing humans completely, AI is more likely to work alongside people, making jobs easier and more efficient.

问题: What is the main idea of the passage?

options.A: AI will completely replace human workers in all fields.
options.B: AI is useful but works best with human creativity and emotion.
options.C: AI is too dangerous to be used in daily life.
options.D: AI has no real impact on jobs or society.

analysis: 文章指出AI虽然能帮助人类完成重复性工作，但缺乏人类的创造力和情感理解，因此更可能与人类协作而非完全取代人类。选项B准确概括了这一核心观点。A与文中观点相反；C和D在文中未提及或与内容不符。

content:
阅读材料：
Have you ever wondered why your reflection in a spoon appears upside down? This phenomenon is due to the unique way concave mirrors work. A concave mirror curves inward, like a cave. When light rays from an object hit such a mirror, they reflect and converge at a point called the focal point. If the object is beyond this focal point, the reflected rays cross over, creating an inverted (upside-down) image. This is why your reflection in the bowl of a spoon is flipped. Interestingly, if you move the spoon very close to your face, the image becomes upright and magnified—this occurs when the object is within the focal point. Concave mirrors are used in telescopes and car headlights to focus light.

问题：
According to the passage, when does a concave mirror produce an upright image?

options.A: When the object is beyond the focal point.
options.B: When the object is exactly at the focal point.
options.C: When the object is within the focal point.
options.D: When the mirror is curved outward.

analysis: 解析：文章明确说明：'if you move the spoon very close to your face, the image becomes upright and magnified—this occurs when the object is within the focal point.'（如果将勺子靠近脸，图像会变成正立且放大的——这发生在物体位于焦点以内时）。因此，当物体在焦点以内时，凹面镜产生正立像。选项A描述的是倒立像的情况；选项B未提及具体效果；选项D描述的是凸面镜，与问题无关。

content:
阅读材料：
Thomas Edison was a famous American inventor. He was born in 1847. As a child, he was very curious and loved to ask questions. His teacher thought he was not smart, but his mother believed in him and taught him at home. Edison worked hard and never gave up. He tried thousands of times before he invented the light bulb. He once said, "I have not failed. I've just found 10,000 ways that won't work." His story teaches us that hard work and persistence are important for success.

问题：
What did Edison's mother do when his teacher thought he was not smart?

options.A: She sent him to another school.
options.C: She asked the teacher to help him more.
options.D: She told him to stop asking questions.

analysis: 根据文章第三句“His teacher thought he was not smart, but his mother believed in him and taught him at home.”可知，当老师认为爱迪生不聪明时，他的母亲选择在家亲自教他。因此正确答案是B。

content:
阅读材料：
Have you ever wondered why some animals can see in the dark while humans cannot? The secret lies in a special layer behind their retinas called the tapetum lucidum. This layer acts like a mirror, reflecting light that passes through the retina back onto the light-sensitive cells. This gives the cells a second chance to detect the light, significantly improving night vision. Animals like cats, dogs, and deer possess this feature, which is why their eyes seem to glow when light shines on them in the dark. However, this enhanced night vision comes at a cost: animals with a tapetum lucidum generally have slightly blurrier daytime vision because the reflected light can scatter.

问题：
According to the passage, what is the main trade-off for animals having a tapetum lucidum?

options.A: They lose their ability to see colors.
options.B: Their daytime vision becomes less sharp.
options.C: They become sensitive to bright sunlight.
options.D: Their eyes take longer to adjust to darkness.

analysis: 解析：文章最后一句明确指出：'However, this enhanced night vision comes at a cost: animals with a tapetum lucidum generally have slightly blurrier daytime vision because the reflected light can scatter.'（然而，这种增强的夜视能力是有代价的：拥有明毯的动物通常在白天视力会稍微模糊一些，因为反射的光线可能会散射。）这直接对应选项B（它们的白天视力变得不那么清晰）。选项A、C、D在文中均未提及。

content:
The Dragon Boat Festival, celebrated on the fifth day of the fifth lunar month, is one of China's most important traditional festivals. Its origin is often linked to the memory of the patriotic poet Qu Yuan from the Warring States period. People throw zongzi, sticky rice dumplings wrapped in bamboo leaves, into rivers as an offering. The most exciting event is the dragon boat race, where teams paddle colorful boats decorated like dragons to the beat of drums. This festival is not only about remembering history but also about promoting community spirit and warding off evil for good health.

Question: What is the main purpose of the dragon boat race during the Dragon Boat Festival?

options.A: To compete for prize money and trophies.
options.B: To remember Qu Yuan and promote teamwork.
options.C: To sell zongzi to the audience.
options.D: To practice for international sports competitions.

analysis: 解析：文章明确指出赛龙舟是端午节最激动人心的活动，并提到这个节日不仅是为了纪念历史（即屈原），也是为了弘扬社区精神（community spirit）。选项B准确概括了这两层目的。选项A（争夺奖金）和D（为国际比赛训练）在文中未提及；选项C（向观众卖粽子）与文章描述的向河中投粽子祭祀的行为不符。

content:
— I heard you're going to the science fair this weekend. ______
— Actually, I've changed my mind. The weather forecast says it'll be pouring, and I don't want to get caught in the rain.

options.A: How come you decided to go there?
options.B: Would you like me to give you a lift?
options.C: What's your plan for the preparation?
options.D: You're not backing out, are you?

analysis:
本题考查在真实对话语境中根据答语反推问句的逻辑推理能力。答句的关键信息是“Actually, I've changed my mind”（实际上我改主意了），并解释了改变主意的原因。因此，问句应当包含对“改变原计划/退出”的推测或质疑。选项D“You're not backing out, are you?”（你不是要打退堂鼓吧？）使用反意疑问句表达对对方可能取消计划的猜测，与答句的“changed my mind”直接对应，构成逻辑闭环。

其他选项分析：
A项询问决定去的原因，但答句并未解释当初为何决定去，而是解释为何不去，故不匹配。
B项提出搭车建议，答句未涉及交通方式，且与“改主意”无关。
C项询问准备计划，答句焦点是取消参与，而非准备细节。
本题难点在于：1. 需通过答句反推隐含的对话前提；2. 掌握“back out”这一短语动词在口语中的含义（退出、食言）；3. 理解反意疑问句在对话中表达推测的语用功能。

content: — ______ is the weather like today? — It's sunny and warm.
analysis: 本题考查日常对话中询问天气的固定句型。询问天气有两种常见句型：1. "What is the weather like?" 2. "How is the weather?" 本题题干中已有 "like"，因此必须用 "What" 提问，构成固定搭配 "What is...like?"。选项A "How" 虽然可以询问天气，但需去掉 "like"；选项C "Why" 询问原因；选项D "When" 询问时间，均不符合语境。

content: — I'm sorry I forgot to bring your book today. — ______. You can bring it next time.
analysis: 本题考查道歉情境下的交际用语。当对方道歉时，常用 "That's all right" 或 "It doesn't matter" 表示原谅。但本题后半句 "You can bring it next time" 暗示说话者接受了道歉并提出了解决方案，因此选用更自然的 "That's all right"（没关系）。"Don't mention it" 和 "You're welcome" 用于回应感谢，不适用于道歉情境。"It doesn't matter" 虽可表原谅，但更侧重“事情不重要”，而 "That's all right" 在口语中更常用且贴合语境。

content: — Can you ______ the book on the desk for me? It's too high. — Sure, here you are.
analysis: 本题考查动词词义辨析。题干意为：“你能帮我把桌上的书拿下来吗？它太高了。”根据语境“It's too high（太高了）”可知，说话者需要对方帮忙“够到”或“拿到”书。选项A reach 意为“伸手够到、拿到”，符合语境。选项B watch（观看）、C read（阅读）、D draw（画画）均与“拿高处物品”的语境不符。

content: Which of the following words has a different sound of the underlined part?
analysis: 本题考查字母组合“ough”的发音辨析。A项though、B项enough、C项through中划线部分均发/ʌ/音，而D项rough中划线部分发/ʌf/音（其中“ou”发/ʌ/，但整体发音包含尾音/f/）。本题难点在于“ough”组合在英语中有多种发音（如through中发/uː/，though中发/əʊ/等），但选项中A、B、C三词在常见发音变体中“ou”部分均含/ʌ/音（enough实际发音为/ɪˈnʌf/），而rough/ rʌf /的发音结构与前三项有明显区别。此题通过对比常见易混词，考查学生对不规则发音的辨析能力。

content: Which of the following words has a different sound of the underlined part from the others?
analysis:
本题考查元音字母组合“ea”的发音。A选项cheap中“ea”发/iː/；B选项break中“ea”发/eɪ/；C选项head中“ea”发/e/；D选项healthy中“ea”发/e/。题目要求找出划线部分发音与其他三项不同的单词，B选项break的发音/eɪ/与其他三项的/iː/或/e/均不同，但需注意：本题实际考查的是四个单词中“ea”的发音对比。经核对：A(/iː/)、B(/eɪ/)、C(/e/)、D(/e/)，因此发音不同的是B。但若按常见中考题设计，常将break作为特例（ea发/eɪ/），而其他三个发/e/或/iː/。但本题选项中，head与healthy实际均发/e/，cheap发/iː/，break发/eɪ/，三者各不相同。若按常见分类，break的发音是唯一不同的。但严谨来看，四个单词发音均不同：A/iː/、B/eɪ/、C/e/、D/e/，其中C和D相同，A和B均不同。因此答案应为B。但根据题干“different from the others”，应选与其他三个不同的一个：若以多数发音为参照，head和healthy都发/e/，cheap发/iː/，break发/eɪ/，没有唯一多数群，此题设计存在歧义。典型中考题通常设三个相同发音，一个不同。调整后常见正确题例为：A. head B. bread C. break D. breakfast，答案选C（break中ea发/eɪ/，其他发/e/）。本题原选项设置不够严谨，但若强制选择，根据常见考试模式，应选B（break发音最特殊）。然而提供的选项答案标记为C，可能原题意图是考查head中ea发/e/，其他三个发/iː/？但实际healthy也发/e/。因此本题需修正。

修正建议：若答案为C，则预设A、B、D中ea发/iː/，但break实际发/eɪ/，不符合。因此本题作为单选题存在缺陷。

为符合中考标准，提供解析如下：按常见中考题库，break中“ea”发/eɪ/，属于特例；cheap、healthy中“ea”通常发/iː/（但healthy实际发音为/ˈhelθi/，其中ea发/e/），因此本题设计不严谨。建议使用以下选项：A. meat B. seat C. great D. eat，答案选C（great中ea发/eɪ/，其他发/iː/）。

鉴于用户要求返回原格式，仍按原选项和给定答案C输出，但说明实际考查点：head中“ea”发/e/，而cheap发/iː/，break发/eɪ/，healthy发/e/。若以head为答案，则预设其他三个发音相同，但实际并不相同，故本题需优化。

content: Which word has a different sound of the underlined part?
analysis: 本题考查元音字母a在开音节和闭音节中的发音。A、B、D选项中的a均处于开音节（或相对开音节）中，发/eɪ/音；而C选项cat中的a处于闭音节中，发/æ/音。因此C选项的发音与其他三项不同。

content: Which word has a different sound of the underlined part?
analysis: 本题考查元音字母a在开音节和闭音节中的发音规则。选项A、B、D中的a均处于开音节结构（以“元音字母+辅音字母+e”结尾或直接以元音结尾），发/eɪ/音；而选项C中的a处于闭音节结构（以辅音字母结尾），发/æ/音。因此C项的发音与其他三项不同。

content: Which of the following words has a different sound of the underlined part?
analysis: 本题考查元音字母组合 ea 的发音。选项 B、C、D 中 ea 均发 /e/（短音），如 head /hed/，healthy /ˈhelθi/，bread /bred/。而选项 A 中 cheap 的 ea 发 /iː/（长音），读音为 /tʃiːp/。因此 A 项发音与其他三项不同。

content: Which of the following words has a different sound of the underlined part?
analysis: 本题考查元音字母组合“ea”的发音。A项cheap中ea发/iː/，C项head中ea发/e/，D项healthy中ea发/e/，而B项bread中ea发/e/。但本题问的是“不同发音”，实际上A项发音与其他三项不同。然而，经核查发音标准：A /tʃiːp/（长音iː），B /bred/（短音e），C /hed/（短音e），D /ˈhelθi/（短音e）。因此B、C、D发音相同（/e/），A不同。故正确答案为A。但根据常见中考题设置，本题实际意图是考查ea发/e/时的例外词，bread常被作为范例，且head、healthy中ea也发/e/，而cheap中ea发/iː/。因此答案应为A。但若按选项标注的“划线部分”对应位置（ea），则A的ea发/iː/，其余发/e/，故A是不同项。最终确定答案为A。

content: Which word has a different sound of the underlined part?
analysis: 本题考查元音字母a在单词中的发音。选项A、B、D中，a均发短元音/æ/；而选项C中的a在“cake”中发长元音/eɪ/，与其他三个单词的发音不同。

content: Which word has a different sound of the underlined letter?
analysis: 本题考查元音字母a在单词中的发音。选项A、B、D中的a均发短元音/æ/，而选项C中的a在开音节结构（辅音+元音+辅音+e结尾）中发双元音/eɪ/，与其他三项发音不同。

content: Which word has a different sound of the underlined letter?
analysis: 本题考查元音字母a在单词中的发音。选项A、B、D中字母a均发短元音/æ/，而选项C中字母a在开音节结构（辅音+元音+辅音+e）中发双元音/eɪ/，与其他三项发音不同。

content: Which word has a different sound of the underlined letter?
analysis: 本题考查元音字母a在单词中的发音。选项A、B、D中字母a均发短元音/æ/；选项C中字母a在“face”中发/eɪ/，与其他三个单词的发音不同。

content: The new sports center ______ in our town next year. It will be open to all citizens.
analysis: 本题考查一般将来时的被动语态。题干中时间状语为“next year”，表示将来，且主语“The new sports center”是动作“建造”的承受者，因此需用一般将来时的被动语态“will be + 过去分词”。选项B为一般现在时被动，C为一般过去时被动，D为现在完成时被动，均与时间状语不符。

content: The classroom ______ by the students every day.
analysis: 本题考查一般现在时的被动语态。主语 'The classroom' 是动作的承受者，且句末有 'every day' 表示日常习惯，因此需用一般现在时的被动语态：am/is/are + 过去分词。主语为单数，故用 is cleaned。选项 B、C、D 均为主动语态或时态错误。

content: The new library in our city ______ by the end of next month.
analysis: 本题考查被动语态与将来时态的结合。题干中的时间状语“by the end of next month”表示“到下个月末为止”，强调在将来某一时间点之前完成的动作，因此需用将来时态的被动语态（will be + 过去分词）。选项B为一般现在时被动，选项C为现在完成时被动，选项D为一般过去时被动，均与将来时间状语不符。

content: The new science museum in our city ______ by thousands of visitors since it opened last month.
analysis:
本题考查现在完成时的被动语态。句意：“我市的新科技馆自上个月开放以来，已经被数千名游客参观过。”
关键提示词是“since it opened last month”，表示从过去某个时间点持续到现在的动作，强调对现在的影响或结果，因此需用现在完成时。主语“museum”与动词“visit”之间为被动关系，故用现在完成时的被动语态：have/has been + 过去分词。主语为单数，因此正确答案为A。
B选项为一般过去时的被动语态，仅表示过去发生的动作，与“since”时间状语不符；C选项为一般现在时的被动语态，表示经常性动作，不符合语境；D选项为一般将来时的被动语态，与时间状语矛盾。

content: The new science museum in our city ______ by thousands of visitors since it opened last month.
analysis:
本题考查现在完成时的被动语态。句意：“我市的新科技馆自上个月开放以来，已经被数千名游客参观过。”
关键时间提示词为“since it opened last month”，表示从过去某个时间点持续到现在的动作，强调对现在的影响或结果，应使用现在完成时。主语“museum”与动词“visit”之间为被动关系，故用现在完成时的被动语态：have/has been + 过去分词。主语为单数，因此正确答案为A。
B选项为一般现在时被动语态，表示经常性动作，与“since”时间状语不符；C选项为一般过去时被动语态，仅表示过去发生的动作，无法体现“持续至今”；D选项为一般将来时被动语态，与句意时间矛盾。

content: The classroom ______ every day by the students.
analysis: 本题考查一般现在时的被动语态。主语“教室”是被打扫的对象，因此需用被动语态。句末的“every day”提示动作是经常性发生的，应用一般现在时。被动语态结构为“be + 过去分词”，一般现在时中be动词用is/am/are。主语classroom是单数，故用is cleaned。选项A为主动语态，C缺少be动词，D为过去时，与every day不符。

content: The classroom ______ by the students every day.
analysis: 本题考查一般现在时的被动语态。主语“教室”是动作“打扫”的承受者，需用被动语态。根据时间状语“every day”判断为一般现在时，被动结构为“am/is/are + 过去分词”。主语classroom为单数，故用is cleaned。B、C、D选项分别为主动语态、过去时和进行时，均不符合语境。

content: The classroom ______ by the students every day.
analysis: 本题考查一般现在时的被动语态。主语 'The classroom' 是动作的承受者，且句末有 'every day' 表示日常习惯，因此需用一般现在时的被动语态：am/is/are + 过去分词。主语为单数，故正确答案为 A 'is cleaned'。B 和 D 为主动语态，C 为一般过去时，均不符合语境。

content: The classroom ______ by the students every day.
analysis: 本题考查一般现在时的被动语态。主语“教室”是动作“打扫”的承受者，应用被动语态。根据时间状语“every day”可知为一般现在时，被动结构为“am/is/are + 过去分词”。主语classroom为单数，故用is cleaned。B、C、D选项均为主动语态或时态错误。

content: The classroom ______ by the students every day.
analysis: 本题考查一般现在时的被动语态。句意应为“教室每天被学生们打扫”，主语classroom是动作的承受者，需用被动语态。every day提示时态为一般现在时，被动结构为“am/is/are + 过去分词”，主语classroom为单数，故用is cleaned。B、C、D选项均为主动语态形式，不符合句意。

content: Good health is very important for everyone. To stay healthy, we need to eat balanced meals, exercise regularly, and get enough sleep. However, many teenagers often stay up late playing games or watching videos. This bad habit can make them feel tired the next day and even ____ their health in the long run. So remember, a good night's sleep is not a waste of time—it's a key to a healthier life!
analysis: 本题考查上下文逻辑与动词辨析。前文提到“熬夜玩游戏或看视频”是一种坏习惯，会导致“第二天疲劳”，根据“even（甚至）”可知后果是递进的，因此长期来看会对健康产生负面影响。A选项harm意为“损害”，符合语境；B选项improve（改善）和C选项protect（保护）与坏习惯的后果矛盾；D选项record（记录）不符合语义搭配。故正确答案为A。

content: Protecting our environment is everyone's duty. We can start with small actions in daily life. For example, we should turn off lights when leaving a room to save electricity. We can also ____ plastic bags by using reusable cloth bags when shopping. Another good habit is to separate waste into different bins for recycling. If everyone makes a little effort, our planet will become cleaner and healthier.
analysis: 本题考查语境理解和动词辨析。短文主题是环保行动，空格前提到‘使用可重复使用的布袋购物’，这是一种减少塑料污染的行为。A选项‘reduce’意为‘减少’，符合‘用布袋替代塑料袋以减少使用’的环保逻辑。B选项‘produce’（生产）与环保行为相反；C选项‘collect’（收集）不符合购物场景的常见表达；D选项‘ignore’（忽视）与文意矛盾。因此正确答案为A。

content: Last summer, my family and I went to the beach for our holiday. We spent a whole week there. Every morning, we would walk along the shore and collect beautiful seashells. In the afternoon, we usually swam in the clear blue water or built sandcastles. One day, we decided to try something different. We joined a local boat tour to see dolphins. It was amazing to watch them jump and play in the waves. That evening, we sat around a bonfire, roasted marshmallows, and shared stories. It was such a wonderful experience that we all agreed to ____ again next year.
analysis: 本题考查上下文逻辑推理。短文主要描述了一家人去海滩度假的愉快经历，包括捡贝壳、游泳、看海豚和篝火晚会等。最后一句提到“这是一个如此美好的经历，以至于我们都同意明年再____”，根据前文对海滩活动的详细描述和正面评价，可以推断一家人希望明年重复类似的愉快体验，因此“return to the beach（回到海滩）”最符合语境。A选项“参观博物馆”、B选项“呆在家”和D选项“尝试新城市”均与前文的海滩度假主题不符。

content: My mother always wakes up earlier than anyone else in our family. Every morning, she prepares breakfast and packs lunch boxes for us. One day, I woke up at 5 a.m. and saw her quietly making sandwiches in the kitchen. The light was soft, and her movements were gentle. Suddenly, I realized how much love was hidden in those ____. From that day on, I started to help her in the morning, and we would chat while working together. Those moments became the warmest part of my day.
analysis: 本题考察上下文逻辑与词汇理解。前文详细描述了母亲在清晨（early morning）默默准备食物的场景，作者正是在这个特定时刻感受到母爱。空格前的“those”指代前文描述的整体情境，而非具体的“三明治”（B）、“厨房灯光”（C）或“午餐盒”（D）。选项A“early mornings”概括了母亲日复一日付出爱的时间场景，最符合文章主题——母爱体现在日常清晨的坚持中。

content: Every morning, my father gets up early to prepare breakfast for our family. He believes a good breakfast helps us start the day with energy. Today, he made pancakes and fresh orange juice. While we were eating, he suddenly said, "I almost forgot!" Then he went to the kitchen and came back with a small bowl of blueberries. "These are good for your memory," he explained with a smile. I felt so ____ because he always thinks about our health in such small but caring ways.
analysis: 本题考查上下文逻辑与情感推理。前文描述父亲早起做早餐、特意准备蓝莓并解释其益处，体现父亲对家人健康的关心。后文强调“他总是以如此细小却关怀的方式想着我们的健康”，因此作者的情感应是正向的。A选项“lucky”（幸运的）符合语境，表达因拥有父亲的关爱而感到幸福幸运；B（无聊的）、C（疲惫的）、D（生气的）均为负面情绪，与上下文情感矛盾。

content: Many students think learning English is difficult, but with the right methods, it can become much easier. One useful way is to make a study plan. First, set clear goals for each week. For example, you can decide to learn 20 new words. Second, review what you have learned regularly. This helps you remember things longer. Third, try to use English in daily life. You can watch English movies or talk with friends in English. Most importantly, never give up when you face difficulties. Keep practicing and you will see progress. Remember, success comes from good habits and ____ effort.
analysis: 本题考察上下文逻辑与词汇搭配。前文提到“制定学习计划”“定期复习”“在日常生活中使用英语”以及“遇到困难不放弃”，这些都属于需要长期坚持的学习方法。最后一句总结“成功源于好习惯和____的努力”，空格处应填入与“长期坚持”相关的形容词。A项sudden（突然的）与文意矛盾；C项lucky（幸运的）与学习方法无关；D项careless（粗心的）为负面含义，不符合文意；B项continuous（持续的）既能与“好习惯”并列，又能呼应前文强调的坚持练习，因此为正确答案。

content: Last summer, my family and I went to the beach for our holiday. We spent a whole week there. Every morning, we would walk along the shore and collect beautiful seashells. In the afternoon, we usually swam in the clear blue water or built sandcastles. One day, we decided to try something different. We joined a local boat tour to see dolphins. It was amazing to watch them jump and play in the waves. That evening, we sat around a campfire, sharing stories and ____ marshmallows. It was such a wonderful memory that I will never forget.
analysis: 本题考察动词辨析与语境理解。根据前文“sat around a campfire”（围坐在篝火旁）和宾语“marshmallows”（棉花糖）可知，此处应描述与篝火相关的活动。A选项“planting”（种植）、C选项“drawing”（画画）、D选项“washing”（清洗）均不符合篝火场景的常见活动。B选项“roasting”（烘烤）是篝火旁烤棉花糖的典型表达，且与上下文逻辑连贯，因此为正确答案。

content: Good health is very important for everyone. To stay healthy, we need to eat balanced food, exercise regularly, and get enough sleep. However, many students often stay up late to study. This is not a good habit because it makes them tired the next day. If you want to have more energy, you should go to bed early and ____ for at least eight hours every night. Remember, a healthy body helps you study better and feel happier.
analysis: 本题考察上下文逻辑与固定搭配。前文提到“go to bed early（早睡）”，后文“for at least eight hours every night（每晚至少八小时）”明确指向睡眠时长，构成“sleep for + 时间”的常见表达。其他选项：B（工作）、C（玩耍）、D（阅读）均与“go to bed early”及健康建议的语境不符。

content: Last summer, my family took a trip to the mountains. We drove for hours and finally arrived at a small village. The air was fresh and the view was amazing. In the afternoon, we decided to go hiking. The path was steep, but we encouraged each other to keep going. When we reached the top, we saw a beautiful lake below. It was so quiet that we could only hear the wind and birds. That night, we sat outside and looked at the stars. They were much brighter than in the city. This trip taught me that sometimes the best adventures are ____ and close to nature.
analysis: 本题考查上下文逻辑与形容词辨析。全文描述了一次亲近自然、宁静平和的山区旅行，如“fresh air”“quiet”“close to nature”等关键词都体现旅行的质朴与简单。选项A“simple”符合语境，强调这种亲近自然的旅行方式简单而美好。B“expensive”与文中朴素的山村旅行不符；C“dangerous”与文中平和愉快的氛围矛盾；D“crowded”与“quiet”“only hear the wind”描述的宁静相悖。

content: Every morning when I walk into our school library, I feel a sense of peace. The quiet atmosphere helps me focus on my studies. Last week, our teacher asked us to find information for a project. I spent two hours there, reading books and taking notes. The librarian noticed my efforts and gave me a warm smile. That small gesture made me feel ____ and encouraged me to work even harder. Now, I visit the library every day after class.
analysis: 正确答案是A。根据上下文，图书管理员对'我'的努力报以温暖的微笑，这个小小的举动让'我'感到被认可和重视。'appreciated'（被欣赏/被认可）最符合语境逻辑。B选项'tired'（疲惫的）与'encouraged me to work even harder'矛盾；C选项'bored'（无聊的）与积极的学习情境不符；D选项'worried'（担心的）在文中没有依据。

content: Protecting our environment is everyone's duty. We can start with small actions in our daily life. For example, we should turn off lights when leaving a room to save electricity. We can also ____ plastic bags and use reusable shopping bags instead. Planting trees is another good way to help our planet because trees clean the air and provide homes for animals. If we all do our part, the Earth will become a better place to live.
analysis: 本题考察动词在环保语境中的逻辑搭配。短文主题是日常环保行为，前文提到“关灯节电”，后文说“使用可重复利用的购物袋”。根据环保常识，减少白色污染的正确做法是“拒绝使用塑料袋”，因此A选项refuse（拒绝）符合语境。B选项collect（收集）虽与环保相关，但此处与“使用可重复利用购物袋”无法形成合理衔接；C选项produce（生产）和D选项design（设计）均与环保行为逻辑相悖。

content: Last summer, my family and I went to the beach for a week. We spent our days swimming in the clear blue water and building sandcastles. One afternoon, we decided to try something new. We joined a local boat tour to see dolphins. As the boat moved further from the shore, everyone kept looking at the sea, hoping to catch a ____ of these beautiful animals. Suddenly, a group of dolphins appeared! They jumped and played near our boat. It was an unforgettable experience.
analysis: 本题考察名词辨析与固定搭配。语境为在海上观看海豚，需要选择一个与“看到”相关的词。A选项“glimpse”意为“一瞥”，常用搭配“catch a glimpse of”表示“瞥见”，符合文中“希望看到海豚”的语境。B选项“sound”（声音）、C选项“smell”（气味）、D选项“touch”（触摸）均与视觉观察的语境不符，且不能与“catch a ____ of”构成合理搭配。因此正确答案为A。

content: Last summer, my family and I went on a trip to the mountains. We spent three days hiking and enjoying the fresh air. On the second day, we decided to climb to the top of a hill to watch the sunrise. We woke up very early and started our journey in the dark. It was not easy, but we encouraged each other along the way. When we finally reached the top, the sky began to turn orange and red. The view was so beautiful that it ____ all our tiredness. We felt happy and proud of ourselves. That moment will stay in my memory forever.
analysis: 本题考查动词在语境中的逻辑搭配。前文描述爬山很累，但看到日出美景时，疲劳感应该“消失”或“被驱散”。A项forgot（忘记）主语通常是人；C项brought（带来）与句意相反；D项increased（增加）不符合逻辑；B项removed（消除）最符合“美景驱散疲劳”的语境，且主语“it”（指代美景）与remove搭配合理。

content: Last summer, my family took a trip to the mountains. We packed our bags and drove for hours. When we arrived, the air was fresh and cool. We spent our days hiking on beautiful trails and enjoying the views. One afternoon, we found a small lake. The water was so clear that we could see fish swimming. My sister and I decided to ____ in the lake. It was cold at first, but soon we had a great time splashing around. That night, we sat by the campfire and shared stories. It was a wonderful trip that I will always remember.
analysis: 本题考察上下文逻辑和动词搭配。前文提到“a small lake”和“The water was so clear”，后文提到“It was cold at first”和“splashing around（戏水）”，这些线索都指向在水中的活动。选项A“swim（游泳）”符合语境；B“skate（滑冰）”不适合湖泊场景；C“climb（攀爬）”与湖水无关；D“rest（休息）”虽可能发生，但无法与后文“splashing around”形成合理衔接。因此正确答案为A。

content: Good study habits are important for success. One useful method is to review what you have learned regularly. For example, after each class, you can spend ten minutes going over the key points. This helps your brain ____ the information better. Another tip is to find a quiet place to study without distractions. Remember, small efforts every day can lead to big improvements over time.
analysis: 本题考查语境理解和词汇辨析。短文主题是学习方法，前文提到“课后花十分钟复习重点”，后文说“这能帮助大脑更好地____信息”。根据逻辑，复习的目的是为了巩固记忆，因此应选择表示“记住”的选项。A（忘记）和C（忽略）与复习目的相反；D（重复）是复习的行为方式，但“帮助大脑重复信息”搭配不当且不符合常见表达。B（记住）既符合“复习→巩固记忆”的逻辑链，也与“help your brain...”的常见搭配一致。

content: Good health is very important for everyone. To stay healthy, we need to have good habits. First, we should eat a balanced diet with plenty of fruits and vegetables. Second, regular exercise is necessary. We can walk, run, or play sports. Third, getting enough sleep helps our body and mind rest. Finally, we should keep a positive attitude and ____ with friends and family often. Remember, small daily habits make a big difference in our health.
analysis: 本题考查语境理解和词汇辨析。短文围绕健康习惯展开，最后提到保持积极态度，并常与亲友____。根据上下文，保持健康需要良好的社交关系，因此应选积极意义的动词。A项communicate（交流）符合语境，表示常与亲友沟通有益健康。B项argue（争吵）、C项ignore（忽视）、D项avoid（避免）均与健康生活的积极建议相矛盾。

content: It was the annual school sports day. All students gathered on the playground, excited and ready. Tom was nervous because he was going to run in the 400-meter race. Just before the race started, he saw his best friend, Mike, waving at him from the crowd. Mike shouted, 'You can do it, Tom!' Those words made Tom feel much more ____. He took a deep breath and got into position.
analysis: 本题考察上下文逻辑与词义辨析。前文提到Tom在比赛前很紧张（nervous），而好朋友Mike的鼓励话语'You can do it!'应当带来积极的心理变化。选项A.confident（自信的）符合“从紧张转变为更有信心”的语境。B.lonely（孤独的）与朋友加油的场景矛盾；C.bored（无聊的）与运动比赛的紧张氛围不符；D.angry（生气的）缺乏上下文依据。因此正确答案为A。

content: Last summer, my family took a trip to the mountains. We spent three days hiking and enjoying the fresh air. On the second day, we decided to climb to the top of a hill to watch the sunrise. We woke up very early and started our journey in the dark. It was not easy, but we encouraged each other along the way. When we finally reached the top, the sky began to turn orange and red. The view was so beautiful that it ____ all our tiredness. We felt lucky to share such a wonderful moment together.
analysis: 本题考查动词在语境中的搭配与逻辑。前文提到爬山很累（tiredness），但看到美景后，这种疲惫感应该会“消失”或“被驱散”。A项forgot（忘记）主语通常是人；C项described（描述）和D项recorded（记录）与“疲惫”搭配不合逻辑。B项removed（消除、去除）常与负面感受搭配，如remove pain/tiredness，且能体现“美景消除疲惫”的因果关系，因此为最佳答案。

content: Good health is very important for everyone. To stay healthy, we need to eat balanced food, exercise regularly, and get enough sleep. However, many students often ignore one simple but useful habit—drinking enough water every day. Water helps our body work properly. It keeps our skin clear and helps us think better. If you feel tired or have a headache during study, it might be because you haven't drunk enough water. So, remember to ____ a bottle of water with you and drink often. It's an easy way to take care of your health!
analysis: 本题考察动词在具体语境中的搭配与逻辑。上文提到“记得要……一瓶水在身边并经常喝”，选项A“carry”表示“携带”，符合“随身带水”的日常健康习惯。选项B“buy”（购买）与上下文“经常喝”的持续性建议不符；选项C“fill”（装满）虽可与“bottle”搭配，但“装满一瓶水”不能直接解决“随身带水”的建议；选项D“share”（分享）与个人健康习惯的语境关联较弱。因此，从语义连贯和常识角度，A为最佳答案。

content: Every morning, my little brother Tom has a special routine. He always gets up early and goes to the park near our home. There, he feeds the pigeons with some bread. The birds know him now and fly to him as soon as they see him. Tom says this makes him feel happy and ____ for the new day. This simple habit brings joy not only to him but also to the lovely birds in the park.
analysis: 本题考查语境理解和固定搭配。前文描述汤姆每天早上去公园喂鸽子，这让他感到快乐。空格后是“for the new day”，结合日常生活逻辑，早晨的积极习惯通常让人为一天做好准备。固定搭配“feel ready for...”意为“为……做好准备”，符合语境。A项“excited”常与“about”搭配；C项“worried”和D项“tired”与上文“happy”情感矛盾。因此B为最佳答案。

content: Protecting our environment is everyone's duty. We can start with small actions in daily life. For example, we should turn off lights when leaving a room to save electricity. We can also use reusable bags instead of plastic ones when shopping. Another good habit is to ____ waste paper, bottles and cans so they can be made into new things. If we all do our part, the Earth will become cleaner and healthier.
analysis: 正确答案是A。本题考察环保语境下的动词辨析。1. 语义逻辑：后文提到“so they can be made into new things（以便它们能被制成新物品）”，这明确指向“回收再利用”的概念。2. 词汇搭配：“recycle waste paper/bottles/cans（回收废纸/瓶子/罐子）”是固定环保表达。3. 干扰项分析：B项“collect（收集）”虽与环保相关，但未强调“循环利用”的核心目的；C项“burn（焚烧）”会造成污染，与环保理念相悖；D项“forget（忘记）”完全不符合语境。4. 篇章衔接：该空所在句与前文的“save electricity”“use reusable bags”构成并列的环保建议，recycle是最典型的环保行为之一。

content: Last summer, my family took a trip to the mountains. We packed our bags and drove for hours. When we arrived, the air was fresh and cool. We spent our days hiking on small paths and enjoying the beautiful views. One morning, we decided to visit a famous lake nearby. The water was so clear that we could see fish swimming at the bottom. I will never forget the peace and happiness I felt during that trip. Traveling helps us ____ new places and create wonderful memories.
analysis: 正确答案是A。explore意为“探索”，符合上下文逻辑：旅行帮助人们“探索”新地方并创造美好回忆。B选项forget（忘记）与旅行目的相反；C选项avoid（避免）不符合常理；D选项describe（描述）虽与旅行相关，但不如“探索”更能体现旅行中的主动体验。从前后句“visit a famous lake”和“create wonderful memories”可知，此处应填入积极且与“发现新地方”相关的动词。

content: Protecting our environment is everyone's duty. We can start with small actions in our daily life. For example, we should turn off lights when leaving a room to save electricity. We can also ____ plastic bags and use cloth bags instead when shopping. Recycling paper, bottles and cans is another good habit. If everyone makes a little effort, our planet will become cleaner and healthier.
analysis: 本题考察环保语境下的动词辨析。短文主题是日常环保行为，空格前说“我们可以……塑料袋，改用布袋购物”。根据环保常识，减少白色污染的正确做法是“拒绝使用”塑料袋（refuse plastic bags）。B选项collect（收集）虽然与环保相关，但此处与“改用布袋”的逻辑不符；C选项produce（生产）和D选项design（设计）均与环保倡导的行为相反。因此A为正确答案。

content: Protecting our environment is everyone's duty. Small actions can make a big difference. For example, we can reduce waste by using reusable bags instead of plastic ones. We can also save energy by turning off lights when leaving a room. Recently, our school started a recycling program. Students are encouraged to separate paper, plastic, and glass. This helps to ____ resources and reduce pollution. If we all do our part, the Earth will become a cleaner and healthier place.
analysis: 本题考察上下文逻辑与词汇搭配。前文提到学校开展回收计划（recycling program），并鼓励学生分类纸张、塑料和玻璃。回收的主要目的就是‘重新利用资源’，且空格后宾语为‘resources’。A项‘protect’（保护）虽与环保相关，但与前文‘回收分类’的直接目的不匹配；B项‘waste’（浪费）和D项‘ignore’（忽视）均与文意相反；C项‘reuse’（重新利用）准确体现了回收行为的核心目的，且与‘recycling program’形成直接逻辑呼应，因此为正确答案。

content: Good health is very important for everyone. To stay healthy, we need to eat balanced food, exercise regularly, and get enough sleep. However, many students often stay up late to study. This is not a good habit because it makes them tired the next day. If you want to have more energy, you should go to bed early and ____ for at least eight hours every night. Remember, a healthy body helps you study better!
analysis: 本题考察上下文逻辑与固定搭配。前文提到“go to bed early”（早睡），后文“for at least eight hours every night”（每晚至少八小时）自然衔接“睡眠”时长。且全文主题为健康习惯，睡眠（sleep）是核心要素之一。B（工作）、C（玩耍）、D（阅读）均不符合“早睡”后的连贯动作及健康建议的语境。

content: Every morning, my little brother Tom has a funny habit. When he wakes up, he always looks for his favorite toy rabbit. He carries it around the house and even talks to it during breakfast. One day, the rabbit was missing. Tom looked everywhere but couldn't find it. He started to feel very ____. Then, our dog Max came into the room with something in his mouth. It was the rabbit! Max had taken it to his basket. Tom hugged both Max and the rabbit happily.
analysis: 本题考察上下文逻辑与形容词辨析。前文提到Tom找不到心爱的玩具兔子（was missing, couldn't find it），后文说狗把兔子叼回来时他开心地拥抱它们，因此空格处应表示“找不到心爱物品时的情绪”。A选项sad（伤心的）最符合丢失物品后的自然情绪；B选项excited（兴奋的）与寻找未果的情境矛盾；C选项angry（生气的）程度过强且无上下文支持；D选项surprised（惊讶的）未体现情感连续性。故正确答案为A。

content: Protecting our environment is everyone's duty. Small actions can make a big difference. For example, we should turn off lights when leaving a room to save energy. We can also ____ plastic bags by using cloth bags when shopping. Planting more trees helps clean the air and provides homes for birds. If we all do our part, the Earth will become a greener and healthier place.
analysis: 正确答案是A。解析：1）语境逻辑：前文提到“保护环境是每个人的责任”，后文举例“用布袋代替塑料袋”，这属于减少塑料使用的环保行为；2）词义辨析：reduce意为“减少”，符合环保中“减量”的理念；increase（增加）、collect（收集）、produce（生产）均与“用布袋替代塑料袋”的减塑目的矛盾；3）搭配习惯：reduce plastic bags是常见环保表达，与全文倡导的节约能源、植树等行为构成并列关系。

content: Every morning when I walk into our school library, I feel a sense of peace. The quiet atmosphere helps me focus on my studies. Last week, our teacher asked us to find information for a project. I spent two hours there, reading and taking notes. Suddenly, I realized how much I enjoyed the ____ of learning new things. It wasn't just about finishing homework; it was about the joy of discovery.
analysis: 本题考查名词辨析与上下文逻辑。前文描述在图书馆查阅资料、阅读笔记的过程，后句强调“不仅仅是完成作业，更是发现的乐趣”，说明作者享受的是学习新事物的“过程”而非单纯结果。A项process（过程）符合语境；B项result（结果）与后文“not just about finishing”矛盾；C项difficulty（困难）和D项competition（竞争）均未在文中体现。

content: Last summer, my family and I went camping in the mountains. We set up our tent near a clear lake. During the day, we hiked on forest trails and enjoyed the fresh air. In the evening, we sat around the campfire, telling stories and ____ marshmallows. It was a wonderful way to spend our holiday together, away from the busy city life.
analysis: 正确答案是C。解析：根据上下文，夜晚围坐在篝火旁时，常进行的活动是“烤棉花糖”（roasting marshmallows），这是露营时的典型活动。A选项cooking（烹饪）范围太广，不符合篝火旁的具体动作；B选项eating（吃）虽然可能发生，但缺少“烤”的关键步骤；D选项buying（购买）与情境不符。因此C最符合语境和常识。

content: Protecting our environment is everyone's duty. We can start with small actions in our daily life. For example, we should turn off lights when leaving a room to save energy. We can also ____ plastic bags and use reusable bags when shopping. Planting trees is another good way to help our planet. If everyone makes a little effort, the Earth will become a better place to live.
analysis: 本题考察动词在环保语境中的准确运用。短文主题是日常环保行动，空格前提到“购物时”，空格后是“塑料袋并使用可重复使用的袋子”。根据环保常识，减少白色污染的正确做法是“拒绝使用塑料袋”，因此A选项refuse（拒绝）最符合语境。B选项collect（收集）不符合日常购物场景的逻辑；C选项produce（生产）与环保行动相反；D选项clean（清洁）与上下文语义不连贯。

content: Many students think learning English is difficult, but there are good ways to make it easier. First, you should practice every day. Even fifteen minutes a day can help a lot. Second, try to use English in real life. For example, you can watch English movies or listen to English songs. This makes learning more fun. Third, don't be afraid of making mistakes. Mistakes are a natural part of learning. When you find something hard, just keep trying and never ____. With these methods, you will see progress soon.
analysis: 本题考查动词短语辨析。语境是遇到困难时应该坚持，不要放弃。A项 give up 意为“放弃”，符合句意“继续努力，永不放弃”。B项 give in 意为“屈服，让步”；C项 give away 意为“赠送，泄露”；D项 give out 意为“分发，耗尽”。根据前文“keep trying（继续努力）”的提示，应选择表示“放弃”的短语，故正确答案为A。

content: Good health is very important for everyone. To stay healthy, we need to eat balanced meals, exercise regularly, and get enough sleep. However, many students often stay up late to study. This is not a good habit because it makes them tired the next day. If you want to have more energy, you should go to bed early and ____ for at least eight hours every night. Remember, a healthy body helps you study better!
analysis: 本题考察上下文逻辑与固定搭配。前文提到'go to bed early（早睡）'，后文'for at least eight hours every night（每晚至少八小时）'自然衔接的是睡眠时长。'sleep'与'go to bed'构成完整语义链，且'睡眠八小时'是常见健康建议。其他选项：B（工作）、C（玩耍）、D（阅读）均不符合'每晚八小时'的合理性与健康主题的语境要求。

content: Good health is very important for everyone. To stay healthy, we need to eat a balanced diet with plenty of fruits and vegetables. We should also exercise regularly, such as running, swimming or playing ball games. Besides, getting enough sleep is necessary for our body to rest and recover. However, many students today spend too much time on their phones, which can harm both their eyes and sleep quality. Therefore, we should ____ our screen time and take breaks to protect our health.
analysis: 本题考察上下文逻辑和词义辨析。前文提到“许多学生花太多时间在手机上，这对眼睛和睡眠质量都有害”，因此后文应提出建议“我们应该____使用屏幕的时间”。A选项limit（限制）符合健康建议的逻辑；B选项increase（增加）与健康建议相反；C选项ignore（忽略）和D选项record（记录）均不符合语境。故正确答案为A。

content: Good study habits are important for success. Many students think studying for a long time is the best way, but this is not always true. Taking short breaks during study can actually help you learn better. When you study for too long without a break, your brain gets tired and it becomes harder to remember information. A good method is to study for 25 minutes and then take a 5-minute break. During the break, you can stand up, stretch, or look out of the window. This helps your brain ____ and get ready for more learning. Remember, studying smart is better than just studying hard!
analysis: 正确答案是A。解析：1）上下文逻辑：前文提到“学习25分钟后休息5分钟”，休息时“站起来、伸展或看窗外”都是让大脑放松的活动；2）词汇搭配：大脑“放松”（relax）是常见表达，且与“为更多学习做准备”形成合理因果；3）干扰项分析：B（sleep）在5分钟休息中不现实；C（work）与“休息”矛盾；D（run）与“看窗外”等温和活动不一致。因此选A最符合学习方法的科学描述。

content: Last summer, my family took a trip to the mountains. We packed our bags and drove for hours. When we arrived, the fresh air and green trees made us feel relaxed. We stayed in a small wooden house near a lake. Every morning, we would walk around the lake and enjoy the beautiful view. One day, we decided to climb to the top of a hill. It was tiring, but when we reached the top, we saw a wonderful rainbow in the sky. That moment, I realized that traveling is not just about visiting new places, but also about ____ special memories with loved ones.
analysis: 本题考查语境理解和动词辨析。短文主题是旅行带来的美好体验，最后一句意为“旅行不仅是参观新地方，更是与所爱之人____特殊回忆”。根据上文描述的爬山、看彩虹等愉快经历，此处应表达“创造回忆”。A选项creating（创造）符合积极语境；B选项forgetting（忘记）与文意相反；C选项copying（复制）和D选项breaking（破坏）均不符合旅行积累回忆的常理。

content: Last summer, my family and I went camping in the mountains. We set up our tent near a clear lake. During the day, we hiked on forest trails and enjoyed the fresh air. In the evening, we sat around the campfire, telling stories and ____ marshmallows. It was a wonderful way to spend our holiday together, away from the busy city life.
analysis: 正确答案是C。解析：1）语境线索：前文提到“sat around the campfire”（围坐在篝火旁），这是露营时的典型活动；2）搭配习惯：“roast marshmallows”（烤棉花糖）是英语中描述露营时用火烤棉花糖的固定搭配；3）逻辑排除：A项“cooking”泛指烹饪，但烤棉花糖通常不用cook；B项“eating”只强调吃，但空格处需要描述围坐篝火时的动作；D项“buying”与露营场景不符。因此C最符合上下文语境和英语表达习惯。

content: At our school, we have a special tradition called "Friendship Week." During this week, students are encouraged to make new friends and show kindness to others. Last year, I decided to join the activity by sitting with someone who was eating alone in the cafeteria. To my surprise, we quickly found common interests in music and basketball. Now, that person has become one of my closest friends. This experience taught me that a small act of courage can ____ a meaningful friendship.
analysis: 正确答案是A。解析：1. 上下文逻辑：前文描述通过主动结交新朋友的行为，后文说“建立了深厚的友谊”，因此空格应表示“建立/创造”。2. 词义辨析：A.create（创造/建立）符合“建立友谊”的搭配；B.forget（忘记）与文意相反；C.break（打破）为负面含义，与故事积极主题不符；D.hide（隐藏）不符合语境。3. 固定搭配："create a friendship"是常见英语表达，意为“建立友谊”。

content: Protecting our environment is everyone's duty. We can start with small actions in daily life. For example, we should turn off lights when leaving a room to save electricity. We can also use reusable bags instead of plastic ones when shopping. Another good habit is to ____ waste paper, bottles and cans so they can be made into new things. If we all do our part, the Earth will become cleaner and healthier.
analysis: 正确答案是A。解析：1）语境线索：后文提到“so they can be made into new things”（以便它们能被制成新物品），这明确指向“回收再利用”的概念。2）词义辨析：A.recycle专指“回收再利用”；B.collect（收集）未体现再利用目的；C.burn（焚烧）不符合环保主题；D.forget（忘记）与文意矛盾。3）环保常识：回收废纸、瓶罐是常见的环保行为，与短文主题完全契合。

content: My little brother Tom is always full of energy. Every morning, he jumps out of bed and runs to the kitchen for breakfast. He enjoys ____ with our dog in the yard before school. This daily routine makes him happy and ready for the day. Sometimes, I join them, and we all have a great time together.
analysis: 本题考查上下文逻辑和动词搭配。根据上文“full of energy（充满活力）”和“runs to the kitchen（跑向厨房）”可知汤姆活泼好动；下文“with our dog in the yard（和狗在院子里）”和“join them（加入他们）”进一步提示，与狗在院子里的常见活动是“玩耍（playing）”。其他选项：A“学习”、C“烹饪”、D“阅读”均不符合与狗在院子里的活动场景，且与上下文描述的活泼氛围不一致。

content: Last summer, my family and I went camping in the mountains. We set up our tent near a clear lake. During the day, we hiked on forest trails and enjoyed the fresh air. In the evening, we sat around the campfire, telling stories and ____ marshmallows. It was a wonderful way to spend our holiday together, away from the busy city life.
analysis: 正确答案是C。解析：1）语境线索：前文提到“sat around the campfire”（围坐在篝火旁），篝火露营时典型的活动是“烤棉花糖”（roast marshmallows），这是英语中固定搭配的露营活动。2）词义辨析：A（cooking）泛指烹饪，但烤棉花糖通常用roast更准确；B（eating）只强调“吃”，但空格处需要描述围绕篝火进行的动作；D（buying）不符合露营场景的逻辑。3）文化常识：在英语国家，露营时“roast marshmallows”是常见假日活动，符合初中生知识范围。

content: Last summer, my family took a trip to the mountains. We drove for hours until we reached a small village. The air was fresh and clean, and the view was amazing. We decided to go hiking the next morning. The path was steep but we enjoyed every step. When we finally got to the top, we saw a beautiful lake below. It was so quiet that we could hear the birds singing. That moment made me realize how wonderful it is to ____ nature. I will never forget this trip.
analysis: 正确答案是A。解析：1）语境线索：前文描述爬山、到达山顶、看到湖泊等，都属于“探索自然”的过程；2）逻辑关系：作者通过旅行感受到自然的美好，因此应表达积极体验，B（忘记）、C（避开）与文意矛盾，D（改变）不符合旅行体验的描写重点；3）固定搭配：'explore nature'（探索自然）是常见英语表达，符合初中词汇要求。

content: Every morning when I walk into our school, I feel a sense of belonging. The school is not just a place for studying; it is also where we grow, laugh, and make memories. Last week, our class organized a charity sale to help children in need. We prepared handmade crafts, books, and snacks. During the event, everyone worked together enthusiastically. What impressed me most was seeing how our small efforts could ____ a big difference. This experience taught me that kindness starts from our own actions, right here in our school community.
analysis: 本题考查固定搭配。'make a difference' 是英语中常见短语，意为'产生影响/起作用'，符合上下文'我们微小的努力能产生很大影响'的语义。其他选项：'take a difference'（无此搭配），'have a difference'（意为'有分歧'，语义不符），'do a difference'（搭配错误）。

content: Good health is very important for everyone. To stay healthy, we need to eat balanced food, exercise regularly, and get enough sleep. However, many students often ignore one simple but useful habit—drinking enough water every day. Water helps our body work well. It keeps our skin healthy and helps us think clearly. When we feel tired or have a headache, sometimes it is just because we don't drink enough water. So, remember to ____ a bottle of water with you and drink often, especially when you study or play sports.
analysis: 本题考察动词在具体语境中的搭配与逻辑。短文主题是提醒学生养成随身带水、经常喝水的好习惯。空格前“remember to”表示“记得要做某事”，空格后是“a bottle of water with you”。A选项“carry”意为“携带”，符合“随身带水”的语境。B选项“buy”（购买）虽然可以接“a bottle of water”，但上下文没有强调购买行为；C选项“fill”（装满）通常用于“fill the bottle with water”，与后文“with you”搭配不当；D选项“share”（分享）与文中强调的个人健康习惯逻辑不符。因此，只有A选项使句意通顺合理。

content: Every morning, my family has a special routine. We sit together at the breakfast table and share our plans for the day. My father always drinks coffee while reading the news. My younger brother usually eats cereal quickly because he doesn't want to be late for school. My mother prepares sandwiches for our lunch boxes. This morning time helps us feel connected before we all ____ to work, school, or other activities. I think starting the day like this is very important for a happy family.
analysis: 正确答案是A。解析：1）上下文逻辑：前文描述全家人在早餐桌旁分享计划，后文提到“去工作、上学或其他活动”，自然衔接“外出（go out）”的动作。2）短语搭配：“go to work/school”是固定搭配，此处“go out to...”表示“外出前往...”。3）选项辨析：B“stay up”意为“熬夜”，与早晨场景矛盾；C“get up”意为“起床”，但前文已描述在吃早餐，起床动作应更早发生；D“look up”意为“查阅、抬头看”，不符合语境。4）生活常识：早餐后家庭成员通常各自外出开始一天的活动。

content: Protecting our environment is everyone's duty. We can start with small actions in daily life. For example, turning off lights when leaving a room helps save energy. Using reusable bags instead of plastic ones reduces waste. Also, planting trees is a great way to make the air cleaner. If we all work together, our planet will become a better place ____. Remember, even the smallest effort counts!
analysis: 本题考查动词不定式作定语的用法。空格前的名词“place”表示地点，当用动词不定式修饰地点名词时，若动词是不及物动词，需加上适当的介词使其与名词构成逻辑上的动宾关系。“live”为不及物动词，表示“居住在某个地方”应用“live in + place”。因此“a better place to live in”符合语法和语义要求。选项A缺少介词，选项B和D是现在分词形式，通常表示主动或进行状态，不适合此处表示目的或用途的定语修饰。

content: At our school, we have a special tradition called "Friendship Week." During this week, students are encouraged to make new friends and show kindness to others. Last year, I decided to join the activity by sitting with someone who usually ate lunch alone. To my surprise, we discovered we both loved painting and science fiction movies. Now, that person is one of my closest friends. This experience taught me that small acts of kindness can ____ bring people together and create lasting friendships.
analysis: 正确答案是A。解析：上下文语境表明，作者通过一次简单的善意举动（与独自午餐的同学坐在一起）就结交到了挚友，强调这种善举“容易”拉近人与人之间的距离。B项“突然地”侧重意外性，但文中强调的是过程的自然性；C项“最终”暗示漫长过程，与文中“很快发现共同爱好”不符；D项“安静地”与行为方式相关，但文中重点在行为效果而非方式。"easily" 最贴合 "small acts can... bring people together" 的因果逻辑。

content: Protecting our environment is everyone's duty. We can start with small actions in daily life. For example, turning off lights when leaving a room helps save energy. Using reusable bags instead of plastic ones reduces waste. Planting trees is also important because they clean the air and provide homes for animals. If we all work together, we can ____ a greener and healthier planet for the future.
analysis: 本题考查动词在语境中的逻辑搭配。全文围绕环保行动展开，最后一句表达“如果我们共同努力，就能为未来____一个更绿色、更健康的地球”。A选项create（创造）符合语境，表示通过行动创造更好的环境；B选项forget（忘记）与文意相反；C选项break（破坏）与环保目标矛盾；D选项avoid（避免）后常接负面内容，与“更绿色健康的地球”搭配不当。且create a...planet为常见环保倡议用语，故选A。

content: Last summer, my family took a trip to the mountains. We drove for hours and finally arrived at a small village. The air was fresh and the view was amazing. In the afternoon, we decided to go hiking. The path was steep, but we encouraged each other to keep going. When we reached the top, we saw a beautiful lake below. It was so quiet that we could hear the wind ____ through the trees. That moment made me realize how wonderful nature is.
analysis: 正确答案是A。解析：1）语境匹配：前文提到“wind”（风），风穿过树木最自然的搭配是“blow”（吹）。2）动词辨析：A.blowing（吹）描述风的行为；B.running（跑）通常用于液体或人；C.singing（唱歌）多指有生命的事物；D.falling（落下）常指物体自上而下运动。3）逻辑连贯：文中强调环境的宁静（quiet），风吹过树叶的声音是符合自然场景的轻微声响，而其他选项的声音特征或逻辑关联性较弱。

content: Last summer, my family and I went to the beach for our holiday. We spent a whole week there. Every morning, we would walk along the shore and collect beautiful seashells. In the afternoon, we usually swam in the clear blue water or built sandcastles. One day, we decided to try something different. We joined a local boat tour to see dolphins. It was amazing to watch them jump and play in the waves. That evening, we sat around a bonfire, sharing stories and ____ marshmallows. It was a perfect holiday that I will always remember.
analysis: 本题考查动词辨析和语境理解。根据前文“sat around a bonfire（围坐在篝火旁）”和后文“marshmallows（棉花糖）”可知，篝火旁常见的活动是“烤棉花糖”。roasting意为“烤”，符合语境。painting（绘画）、washing（清洗）、throwing（扔）均与篝火场景和棉花糖的常见活动不符。

content: Good health is very important for everyone. To stay healthy, we need to eat balanced food, exercise regularly, and get enough sleep. However, many students today spend too much time on their phones or computers. This can make them feel tired and even ____ their eyesight. So remember to take breaks and look at things far away during study time. A healthy lifestyle helps us study better and enjoy life more!
analysis: 本题考查上下文逻辑与动词辨析。前文提到“长时间使用手机或电脑会导致疲劳”，根据常识和语境，这种行为通常对视力有负面影响。A项improve（改善）和B项protect（保护）与语境矛盾；D项test（测试）不符合语义逻辑；C项harm（损害）符合“过度使用电子设备损害视力”的常识与上下文因果关系。

content: Last summer, my family and I went camping in the mountains. We spent three days there, enjoying fresh air and beautiful views. On the second day, we decided to hike to a nearby lake. The path was steep, but we encouraged each other along the way. When we finally reached the lake, the clear blue water ____ us feel relaxed and happy. We sat by the lake, had a picnic, and even saw some wild birds. It was a wonderful experience that I will never forget.
analysis:
本题考查动词辨析及使役动词的用法。句意为“清澈的湖水让我们感到放松和快乐”。
- A选项“made”是使役动词，构成“make sb. do sth.”结构，意为“使某人做某事”，符合语境。
- B选项“asked”意为“要求”，常用结构为“ask sb. to do sth.”，但空格后是动词原形“feel”，语法不匹配。
- C选项“helped”意为“帮助”，常用结构为“help sb. (to) do sth.”，虽语法可行，但“湖水帮助我们感到快乐”不如“使/让我们感到快乐”自然贴切。
- D选项“told”意为“告诉”，常用结构为“tell sb. to do sth.”，语法和语义均不符合。
从语法和语境综合判断，A选项为最佳答案。

content: Good study habits are important for success. Many students think studying longer is better, but this is not always true. The key is to study smarter. One useful method is to take short breaks during study time. For example, after 25 minutes of focused study, you can rest for 5 minutes. This helps your brain ____ information better. Also, try to review what you have learned before going to bed. Your brain processes information while you sleep!
analysis: 本题考察上下文逻辑与动词辨析。前文提到“学习更聪明的方法是在学习期间短暂休息”，后文解释“这能帮助你的大脑更好地____信息”。根据常识和语境，适当的休息有助于提高记忆效率，且后文提到“睡前复习”和“睡眠时大脑处理信息”都指向“记忆”这一积极效果。A（忘记）、B（忽略）、D（避免）均与文意相反，故正确答案为C（记住）。

content: My father is a quiet man. He seldom says "I love you" but shows his love through actions. Every morning, he gets up early to prepare breakfast for me. Last winter, I caught a bad cold and had to stay in bed. My father sat by my bed all night, putting a wet towel on my forehead to reduce the fever. When I woke up, I saw him sleeping in the chair, still holding the towel. At that moment, I deeply felt his ____.
analysis: 本题考查上下文逻辑与主题理解。全文围绕父亲“用行动表达爱”展开：开头点明父亲“很少说‘我爱你’，但通过行动表达爱”，后文用准备早餐、彻夜照顾生病的孩子等具体事例印证这一主题。最后一句“那一刻，我深深感受到了他的____”应是对前文所有行为的总结升华，必须与文章核心主题“父爱”一致。A项“沉默”仅描述性格特征，B项“担忧”和D项“疲惫”都是父爱的具体表现层面，而C项“爱”直接呼应主题，概括了所有行为背后的本质情感，符合完形填空的“主题一致原则”。

content: Last summer, my family decided to spend our holiday in the countryside. We wanted to escape the busy city life and enjoy nature. Every morning, we woke up to the sound of birds singing. During the day, we went hiking in the hills and picked fresh fruits from local farms. One afternoon, we visited a small village nearby. The villagers were very friendly and invited us to try their traditional food. That evening, we sat outside, watching the stars and ____ stories. It was a simple but wonderful holiday that we will always remember.
analysis: 本题考察动词辨析与语境理解。根据前文“sat outside, watching the stars”（坐在户外看星星）和后文“stories”（故事）可知，此处描述的是家人一起“讲故事”的温馨场景。telling stories（讲故事）是常见的假日夜晚活动，符合语境。writing（写）、reading（读）、painting（画）虽然都与故事相关，但在此处户外夜晚的集体活动场景中，口头“讲述”故事最自然合理。

content: Last summer, my family and I went camping in the mountains. We set up our tent near a clear lake. During the day, we hiked on forest trails and enjoyed the fresh air. In the evening, we sat around the campfire, telling stories and ____ marshmallows. It was a wonderful way to spend our holiday together, away from the busy city life.
analysis: 正确答案是C。解析：1）语境线索：前文提到“sat around the campfire”（围坐在营火旁），且“marshmallows”（棉花糖）是露营时常见的烧烤食物；2）固定搭配："roast marshmallows" 是英语中描述“烤棉花糖”的标准表达；3）逻辑排除：A（cooking）泛指烹饪，但烤棉花糖通常不用cook；B（eating）只强调“吃”，忽略“烤”的过程；D（buying）与露营场景不符，且与前文活动不连贯。

content: Protecting our environment is everyone's duty. We can start with small actions in daily life. For example, turning off lights when leaving a room helps save energy. Using reusable bags instead of plastic ones reduces waste. Planting trees is also important because they clean the air and provide homes for animals. If we all work together, we can ____ a greener and healthier planet for the future.
analysis: 正确答案是A。解析：1）语境逻辑：前文描述了一系列环保行动（关灯、使用环保袋、植树），最后一句表示“如果我们共同努力，就能为未来____一个更绿色、更健康的地球”，此处应填入积极且符合“建设/创造”含义的动词。2）词义辨析：A.create意为“创造”，B.forget“忘记”，C.break“破坏”，D.avoid“避免”。只有“create”能呼应前文的积极行动并达成“建设美好地球”的目标。3）固定搭配：“create a... planet”是常见表达，强调通过努力实现新状态。

content: Good health is very important for everyone. To stay healthy, we need to eat balanced food, exercise regularly, and get enough sleep. However, many students often stay up late to study. This bad habit can make them tired and weak. If you want to have a strong body and a clear mind, remember to go to bed early and ____ for at least 8 hours every night. Your body needs rest to grow and repair itself.
analysis: 本题考察上下文逻辑与固定搭配。前文提到“go to bed early”（早睡），后文“for at least 8 hours every night”（每晚至少8小时）明确指向睡眠时长，且与主题“健康”中的睡眠建议直接相关。B（工作）、C（玩耍）、D（阅读）均不符合“早睡”后的连贯动作及健康常识。

content: Last summer, my family decided to spend our holiday in the countryside. We wanted to escape the busy city life and enjoy nature. Every morning, we woke up to the sound of birds singing. During the day, we went hiking in the hills and picked fresh fruits from local farms. One afternoon, we visited a small village nearby. The villagers were very friendly and invited us to join their traditional dance. It was a wonderful experience that made our holiday truly special. This trip taught me that the best way to relax is to ____ with simple joys in life.
analysis: 本题考察动词在具体语境中的搭配与逻辑。原文描述假期在乡村体验简单快乐（simple joys），最后一句总结“最好的放松方式是____生活中的简单快乐”。A选项connect with意为“与…联系/连接”，符合“通过简单快乐获得放松”的因果关系。B选项compare with（比较）、C选项compete with（竞争）均与“放松”主题矛盾。D选项complain about（抱怨）虽可接介词about，但语法和语义均不匹配。故A为正确答案。

content: My father is a quiet man. He doesn't talk much, but he always shows his love through actions. Every morning, he gets up early to make breakfast for me. Last winter, I was sick and had to stay in bed. He sat beside me, reading my favorite stories. When I felt better, he taught me how to make paper planes. We flew them in the yard, laughing together. I finally understood that love is not always about words; sometimes it's about the time and effort someone ____ for you.
analysis: 本题考查动词辨析与固定搭配。句意为“爱有时是关于某人为你花费的时间和精力”。spend time/effort (on) for sb. 是固定搭配，意为“为某人花费时间/精力”。pay 常与 money 搭配；take 常用于 It takes sb. time to do sth. 结构；cost 主语通常是物。根据语境和搭配，A 为正确答案。

content: Every morning, my family has a special routine. After breakfast, we always spend ten minutes talking about our plans for the day. This helps us feel organized and connected. My father shares his work schedule, my mother talks about her errands, and I mention my school activities. Sometimes we also ____ small problems or worries. This short morning talk makes our day smoother and happier.
analysis: 正确答案是A。解析：1）上下文逻辑：前文描述家人早晨会谈论每日计划（talking about our plans），后文提到“小问题或担忧”，自然衔接应为“讨论问题”；2）词义搭配：discuss problems是固定搭配，意为“讨论问题”；3）排除其他选项：B（忘记）、C（隐藏）、D（制造）均不符合语境，因为前文强调沟通的积极作用，讨论问题符合主题。

content: Good health is very important for everyone. To stay healthy, we need to have good habits. First, we should eat healthy food like fruits and vegetables. Second, we need to exercise regularly, such as running or playing sports. Third, getting enough sleep is also necessary. Besides these, we should also keep a positive attitude. When we face difficulties, we should try to ____ and find solutions. Remember, a healthy body and a healthy mind work together to make us strong and happy.
analysis: 本题考察在健康话题语境下对逻辑和词义的理解。前文提到“保持积极态度”，后文说“寻找解决方法”，因此空格处应填入与积极应对困难相关的短语。A选项“保持冷静”符合积极应对的逻辑；B选项“快速放弃”和C选项“忽略它们”都是消极态度，与上下文矛盾；D选项“立即求助”虽不算消极，但不如A选项“保持冷静”更能体现独立解决问题的积极心态，且与后文“寻找解决方法”直接衔接更自然。故正确答案为A。

content: Last summer, my family took a trip to the mountains. We spent three days hiking and enjoying the fresh air. On the second day, we decided to climb to the top of a hill to watch the sunrise. We woke up very early and started our journey in the dark. It was not easy, but we encouraged each other along the way. When we finally reached the top, the sky began to turn orange and red. The view was so beautiful that it ____ all our tiredness. We felt lucky to share such a wonderful moment together.
analysis: 本题考察动词在语境中的逻辑搭配。前文提到爬山很累（tiredness），但看到美景后，这种疲惫感应该会“消失”或“被驱散”。A选项forgot（忘记）主语通常是人，不能与it（指代the view）搭配；C选项described（描述）和D选项recorded（记录）不符合“消除疲惫”的语义逻辑。B选项removed（消除、移除）既能与it搭配，又符合“美景驱散疲惫”的语境，是正确答案。

content: My father is a quiet man. He seldom says "I love you", but he shows his love in actions. Every morning, he gets up early to make breakfast for me. On rainy days, he always waits for me at the school gate with an umbrella. Last week, I had a fever. He stayed up all night, putting a wet towel on my forehead. When I woke up, I saw him sleeping in the chair beside my bed. At that moment, I deeply felt his ____.
analysis: 本题考察上下文逻辑与主题理解。全文通过多个事例（做早餐、雨天送伞、彻夜照顾生病的孩子）集中描写父亲用行动表达关爱。最后一句“At that moment, I deeply felt his ____”是作者的情感总结，应呼应文章核心主题“父爱”。A项“沉默”虽符合父亲性格，但并非此刻的核心感受；B项“担忧”和D项“疲惫”都是父爱的具体表现，不能概括全文主旨；C项“爱”最全面、直接地总结了父亲所有行为背后的情感，且与首段“he shows his love in actions”形成首尾呼应。

content: Last summer, my family and I took a trip to the mountains. We spent three days hiking and enjoying the fresh air. On the second day, we decided to climb to the top of a hill to watch the sunrise. We woke up very early and started our journey in the dark. It was challenging, but when we finally reached the top, the view was absolutely breathtaking. The sky turned orange and pink as the sun slowly rose above the clouds. At that moment, I felt a deep sense of ____. It was an experience I will never forget.
analysis: 本题考察上下文逻辑与情感推理。前文描述爬山看日出的美好经历（breathtaking view, beautiful sky），后文强调“永远不会忘记”，因此空格处应表达积极、宁静的情感。A（peace）符合“大自然中感到平静”的语境；B（anger）负面情绪，与场景矛盾；C（confusion）表示困惑，上下文无相关线索；D（tiredness）虽可能符合爬山后的生理状态，但与“难忘体验”的情感升华不符。故A为最佳答案。

content: Last summer, my family took a trip to the mountains. We spent three days hiking and enjoying the fresh air. On the second day, we decided to climb to the top of a hill to watch the sunrise. We woke up very early and started our journey in the dark. It was not easy, but we encouraged each other along the way. When we finally reached the top, the sky began to turn orange and red. The view was so beautiful that it ____ all our tiredness. We felt lucky to share such a wonderful moment together.
analysis: 本题考查动词在语境中的搭配与逻辑。前文提到爬山很累（tiredness），但看到美景后，这种疲惫感应该会“消失”或“被驱散”。A选项forgot（忘记）主语通常是人，不能以“view”为主语；C选项described（描述）和D选项recorded（记录）均不符合“消除疲惫”的语义逻辑。B选项removed（消除、移除）既能与tiredness构成动宾搭配，又能准确表达“美景驱散了疲惫”的因果关系，因此为正确答案。

content: Last summer, my family took a trip to the mountains. We packed our bags and drove for hours. When we arrived, the air was fresh and cool. We spent our days hiking on beautiful trails and taking photos of the scenery. One evening, we sat around a campfire, telling stories and ____ the stars. It was a wonderful experience that brought us closer together. I will always remember this special trip.
analysis: 正确答案是A。根据上下文，晚上围坐在篝火旁，最合理的活动是“看星星”（watching the stars）。B选项“数星星”（counting）虽然可能，但不如“观看”符合一般露营场景；C选项“画星星”（drawing）和D选项“描述星星”（describing）与前后文“讲故事”的轻松氛围不够连贯，且缺乏直接逻辑关联。此处用“watching”最能体现一家人共同欣赏自然景象的温馨场景。

content: My mother always said she didn't like flowers. Every Mother's Day, I would ask if she wanted a bouquet, and she would shake her head. "Save the money," she said. Last year, I secretly put a small pot of sunflowers on her windowsill. The next morning, I saw her smiling while watering them. Later, my father told me, "Your mother keeps every card you've ever made for her." That's when I understood — her love was never about receiving gifts, but about ____ our love in her own quiet way.
analysis: 本题考查上下文逻辑与词汇辨析。前文提到母亲保存着孩子做的所有卡片，且空后是“our love”，结合全文主题——母亲以安静的方式表达爱。A项“隐藏”与保存卡片的行为矛盾；C项“忘记”不符合亲情主题；D项“展示”与文中“quiet way”不一致。B项“treasuring”（珍惜）最能体现母亲将孩子的爱默默珍藏的情感，且与“keeps every card”形成直接呼应。

content: Last summer, my family and I went to the beach for our holiday. We spent a whole week there. Every morning, we would walk along the shore and collect beautiful seashells. In the afternoon, we usually swam in the clear blue water or built sandcastles. One day, we decided to try something different. We joined a local boat tour to see dolphins. It was amazing to watch them jump and play in the waves. That evening, we sat around a campfire, sharing stories and ____ marshmallows. It was such a wonderful memory that I will never forget.
analysis: 本题考查语境理解和动词搭配。根据前文“sat around a campfire”（围坐在篝火旁）和宾语“marshmallows”（棉花糖）可知，此处应描述与篝火相关的活动。选项A“planting”（种植）、C“drawing”（画画）、D“washing”（清洗）均不符合篝火场景的常见活动。选项B“roasting”（烘烤）是篝火旁烤棉花糖的典型表达，且与上下文逻辑一致，因此为正确答案。

content: Last summer, my family and I went camping in the mountains. We set up our tent near a clear lake. During the day, we hiked on forest trails and enjoyed the fresh air. In the evening, we sat around the campfire, telling stories and ____ marshmallows. It was a wonderful way to relax and spend time together away from the busy city life.
analysis: 正确答案是C。解析：1）语境搭配：在篝火旁（around the campfire）与棉花糖（marshmallows）的典型活动是“烤棉花糖”（roast marshmallows），这是英语中常见的固定搭配。2）词义辨析：A选项buying（购买）不符合露营场景；B选项cooking（烹饪）通常指正餐烹饪，不专指烤棉花糖；D选项washing（清洗）完全不符合逻辑。3）文化常识：在欧美露营文化中，roasting marshmallows over a campfire 是经典活动之一。

content: Learning a new language can be challenging, but using the right methods makes it easier. One useful way is to practice regularly. For example, you can try to learn a few new words every day and use them in sentences. Another good idea is to listen to English songs or watch movies with subtitles. This helps you get used to the sounds and rhythms of the language. Most importantly, don't be afraid of making mistakes. Mistakes are part of the learning ____. Just keep trying, and you will see progress over time.
analysis: 本题考察名词在语境中的搭配与逻辑。前文提到“不要害怕犯错”，后句说“错误是学习的一部分”，根据学习常识和上下文逻辑，错误应是学习“过程”中的自然组成部分。A选项process（过程）符合语义，且与全文讨论学习方法的核心主题一致。B选项result（结果）与“犯错是学习的一部分”逻辑矛盾；C选项plan（计划）和D选项exam（考试）均与上下文语境不符。

content: My father is a quiet man. He doesn't talk much, but he always shows his love through actions. Every morning, he gets up early to make breakfast for me. Last winter, I was sick and had to stay in bed. He sat by my bed the whole night, checking my temperature and bringing me water. When I felt better, I saw him sleeping in the chair with tired eyes. At that moment, I deeply understood that love doesn't always need words. Sometimes, it's just a ____ look or a silent company.
analysis: 正确答案是A。根据上下文，作者生病时父亲整夜守在床边，眼神疲惫，这体现了父亲担忧（worried）的心情。B选项“开心的”不符合生病照顾的场景；C选项“奇怪的”和D选项“严肃的”都无法准确表达父亲对生病孩子的关切之情。文中“tired eyes”和“silent company”都暗示了父亲默默担忧的状态。

content: Last summer, my family and I went on a trip to the mountains. We packed our bags with warm clothes and snacks. The journey took about three hours by car. When we arrived, the air was fresh and cool. We decided to go hiking on the first day. The path was steep, but the view from the top was amazing! We saw green valleys and a small river below. On the second day, we visited a local village and tried some traditional food. The people there were very friendly. This trip taught me that traveling is not just about seeing new places, but also about ____ new experiences and cultures.
analysis: 本题考察上下文逻辑和动词辨析。短文主要描述一次愉快的旅行经历，最后一句总结旅行的意义：不仅是看风景，更是'____新的体验和文化'。A选项'forgetting（忘记）'与文意相反；C选项'avoiding（避免）'不符合旅行中主动接触文化的常理；D选项'changing（改变）'虽有一定关联，但不如B选项'享受'更贴合前文描述的积极体验（amazing view, friendly people, traditional food）。且'enjoying new experiences'是英语中常见搭配，最符合语境。

content: Many students think learning English is difficult. However, with the right methods, it can become much easier. One important tip is to practice regularly. For example, you can try to learn a few new words every day and use them in sentences. Another good way is to listen to English songs or watch movies with subtitles. This helps improve your listening skills. Most importantly, don't be afraid of making mistakes. Mistakes are a natural part of the learning ____. Just keep trying, and you will see progress over time.
analysis: 正确答案是A。解析：1）语境线索：前文提到“不要害怕犯错”，后文说“坚持尝试就会进步”，说明这是在描述学习的过程。2）词义辨析：A.process（过程）符合“学习是一个包含犯错、改进的持续过程”的语义；B.result（结果）与“犯错”逻辑不符；C.exam（考试）和D.competition（比赛）均偏离上下文对学习本质的讨论。3）固定搭配：learning process（学习过程）是常见表达。

content: Good health is very important for everyone. To stay healthy, we need to have good habits. First, we should eat a balanced diet with plenty of fruits and vegetables. Second, regular exercise like running or playing sports helps our bodies stay strong. Third, getting enough sleep is also necessary for our minds and bodies to rest. Besides these, we should also keep a positive attitude. When we face problems, try to ____ and find solutions. A happy mind can make us healthier.
analysis: 本题考察上下文逻辑与固定搭配。前文提到“保持积极态度”和“面对问题”，后文说“找到解决方法”，因此空格处应填入与情绪管理相关的短语。A选项“保持冷静”符合语境，且与“积极态度”形成呼应。B选项“吃得更多”与健康饮食建议矛盾；C选项“睡得更久”与前文“充足睡眠”建议重复且不针对解决问题；D选项“跑得更快”偏离了心理层面的建议。故正确答案为A。
